## Memorial Day Severe Weather Possible
The Memorial Day weather is likely to be split today, with cool, pleasant temperatures in the Midwest and Northeast and rain and possible [severe weather](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMHKnTAUhJ8m7iInx-i9LrIoFHd_N6V0KSfm5JoSjST5r_j2xUKf4N_OMMN88_tj_qAQw_76QRub6tO881ki18p53nhLOVB0dNV0WKZlrencC80I2EvoJQ6zoxCvWTfOeGbkrmGjHvqJvYYRmtUAjt53Y2e9GlD32gK6JyinnbW989QEc7dBj4MCeMLY-odSHfDC1mkLTEIDu1BTbv-ksNeVfbzSm3O7pK2JZq31KKL7JnASOJFdboL2lW5b4PTrp8Cp8Jszy5Oprpxl2A5aapEL7zVTlCV93vouP4v8Dy0dXXeypnyJbgpOdN8VohoABmyycccZYgxleAgNr41C_LenfunEt8G_AQAA__9d5YcJ) in Southern states. Areas surrounding cities like Oklahoma City, Little Rock, Memphis, and Fort Worth are at the highest risk of severe weather today, with [possible flooding](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL_GZB4ZD-AhcHAJ2Vx6IWroNvhk6JHdC-LvV7ur-YKNq1Sq9_49xyfEFPflL2zkJfC401kSiVAeN9o4R0gIl_BrIphX4XMvMFpjW21abbsRIaZrbCr0gcjSvSJfu-Zh28b0plp9XSOF0FLvYHKIE9V96GbE3kBwjrCK_mvNtLarjXmY_hZcXd8NzTRhMxkC1RjCKJxv_znuslJIFx-UbzNvVfKryKuo-x9lB2WHk0DWn0zZIXCmGYooO2RaIu-QlB2OiMTKDm8-jXDpd1WHxIxxX3ShgzLpIpy3ogt_yFplj68zphRL51Rjlg1i-v4hv5J3ePsZAAD__4_siCQ), severe storms, and tornadoes. Today will cap off a weekend of festivities across the country, with the National Memorial Day Parade in [Washington, D.C.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAQhJ_GZEamMT8TOLiEbC69EDV2e_DJ2Aha4-XtV4y0T7AddFSqqq_-PecnhhjS6y9uZNjnOVE5IzHTMW-05SNgdHhx3hdCu3Iu6cQZFHRSdRL62WGI16wrZzwRUFuRaQY9QqfVQ1Wr8V6PY3ufH2jpNLY9ejcOCNa2o_ZVMLeb6qBvlBrVo_ZD07SKLC1OL4pQaEUucD7q_zkkXsnHK7_pqG3eqmhW5v0U7R8Bk4CplFIXPNeQXpxTwHSrBEx3hoBJ3Q8aAZOzp_wBlA4vueOBjuQHrazIkrOkr50sC5iqw7i9hBjD2Q9Cq9eGIX4K8K9Wexv4DgAA__-eSoA7), being the largest event on the docket. Forecasts are calling for an overcast parade in D.C., which will include appearances from Kiss rock star Gene Simmons and four Medal of Honor recipients as the Grand Marshals.
## Military Spends Big to Rebuild Ranks
The U.S. military has poured over [$6 billion into recruiting and retention](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1u7CAUhJ8Gd1hwjP8Kitu429usopTWwRzWRNhYQNby20fOK6Se0Yy-7_MxP9AHv7_-40a6uDjvdOZApVCaN9pi8hgsXiUehnBZSzz3jDMIaLloOXSzRR-uWVVWOyKgpiItezVAq8QoqlU3w0LGuY4MNq6VghzZYSBh0SoHZCqv7zXRQieFGMRYu17KRtBCxiojCJkSZH2Jqf6Kfi8ruXDFN6V6iVsV9FrKkVnzj8HEYEKz3AT1K94xg-njyWA6faJnieliMH1nvvngC6aL54P2wjtufAg-7vzAXHjDL8KUuQSQvRrVWCVtj_Ou5K5nSrw29OH3vfxJ2VvDTwAAAP__MleBmg) over the last three years, scrambling to reverse enlistment declines worsened by the COVID-19 pandemic. The Navy, despite being smaller than the Army, led spending efforts, [offering hefty bonuses](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGSpCAQgOGnwWystgXRgOAuMJu7cEOrGdqRLYQpYLV8-62dV9jsj77g_7gvd_LBx-c_2tnUNS2RzxK4Vs7LznvKnoKjq6aXZXpsNZ2x0IKA6gbqhsPiyIdrkY0zKzNy37DptBxRSZig2YyWSk9uUuNIa281DHrFwQL1LNEOyjXe_GigcOgARpjaVXddD_xg66QFJiGBna8pt5_Jx7rxGq50cG4faW-C2Wp9FdH_ETgLnM_zbCMdV-bC-eB3t7sPAuf_0SbKzsenwPlvil-Fi8C5yca9Th-CL4MWEp47-fC266-GHAa_AwAA___HsHWW) to both officers and enlisted sailors, with some incentives topping $150,000. The Army’s biggest comeback move was the launch of the [Future Soldier Prep Course in 2022](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzz2O6yAUQOHVQBfrgvmJC4rXpMtrp0RgLjEjbCIgQd79KLOF6Y8-6Xzd7d2lnI7Hf7ej6bHYA0fL2DtWu-NeanI5uLOXp0e3br2MoznLgcsLyAtXNriUTytoMBGR40zRMC2uXApYgG5GRaUWLb30MGt3FSIo5nFBt-oFGKw0mY8GkisGcIVlipqxGXBFH4QHdEQAhtRLnb5LOvqGMZ_ljXVay06z2Xp_NjL_I_xG-G2MMcVXf1VsJYeEtX0ywm-0mvAcKefUlCYCHrtL-Zfof_p-G_4TAAD__-WnbPg). This course helps borderline candidates meet fitness and academic standards. It now boasts a 95% graduation rate and has sent nearly 25,000 recruits to basic training. Meanwhile, across all branches, pandemic-era setbacks, like school closures and rising youth disqualification rates, continue to shape a more expensive, more aggressive fight for manpower.
## Top Beach Towns to Visit or Buy In
Realtor.com’s 2025 list of the [most affordable beach towns](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OqzAQhZ_GdI6GsflJ4eI2dLntlmiwh-CVwQiPEvH2K6J9g21Oc6Tv6Dtfj_FBMcXt-Z9WdjLnceN3SSzCx7jymo9IKdApeZ-Y_CL5vRUaEbDR0Ghsx0AxnaOtgpuZkU3Fru5sj42FO1SLg65rjPcejOnQ93UI3k5zbXsDLXSGquguGjTY1gA93G9zV9cG2PMU7ARMygKHKPm4fee4ycJzOvOLj5vPa5XcIrIXZf4pHBQO27nnIlelcLjACge4Aq3C4WBKmouQsMJBFtZrLqJpnvMRaEqsP5b6o6njpn8J1eHC_o4pxdJ2ysJzpZg--_Kn014OfwIAAP__Bc1-rQ) for homebuyers ranks Pascagoula, Mississippi, at No. 1, with a median list price of $165,610. Other top entries include Deerfield Beach, Florida ($211,750), and [Surfside Beach, South Carolina](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL_GdKBlsYEULq6hy7VXImMvYU82jmwniL8_hV-4bqQZzYzez32-G_a8P75NIF3WOO90ZE-lUJoDhZjYeGfOEp8LGbuVeOzZzAioalA19rMz7M9ZVk6vREhdRbod5IhKwg2qTXdu6cdBWmwttMttRIXKjVIqNVhYJVasP22gsG8BRrg169C2HZClxckFyAgJ5LjE1PxG3stGqz_jm1JjY6i83kp5ZtF9CZwETsdxNG_OXMKZiqfr9CcocLo05drGEF47F6YscMqvtGZ2VF92lbR7Huw9534QEh7BsL-Gyr_ovDX-BQAA___z3nrm) ($310,925). If you’re not in the market to buy a beach property, just want to enjoy a perfect beach, [this year’s top-rated](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykjzmO7CAUAE8DWVvPmMUOCH7SWf90QsTysBlhY2HUyLcfTV9hDlClqq-XedmU07H-tzvqFos5sF8ZW8NqdtxLTTYHe7dyOrR-a6UflzUMmHiAeDBpgk35NpwGHREZThT1qPjMBIcF6KYd-mWW3iEwJZT0Kk6SOy5GFRcuYKZJ_9pAMDkCzLAMUY3jBOjRBe4ALeGAIbVSh--SjrZhzHd5Yx182WnWW2vnRaZ_hD0Je_beh1A_rUOpK2FPWnU4e8o5XVIRDutuU_6w7U_Db81-AgAA__-efGmn) list includes Coopers Beach in Southampton, New York, along with Beachwalker Park in South Carolina and Delnor-Wiggins Pass in Florida.

No Heart Left Unreached.** Right now, you have a limited-time opportunity to help [_reach children with the Gospel in the hardest-to-reach places_](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLvOnDAQRp_GdIuGsbkVLiLx022KNCmRsccwkS8IvLvat482yRukPdJ39On8vC93w4HT9t1E0sXnJdHrClQKnUukmE82wZl3ycdKxu4lv9JlFgRsb9DesFuc4fBeVOW0J0KSFemmVwO2Ckaodr0q66RvLPh2QBzX1fvRqxXAd11j-rFi_bFBi10DMMBY-75pJJCl1akVyAgF5Ljks_6VOZWdfHjnJ521zbEKei_luIT8JnAWOG_85LTVOdGeD6oTFYHzYTYSOGN7S9nuHJzP55ZLoRStkPOjxOXKj9OSkNP8Vy6w--BIjh9RyOkrGg7_oDXxMLwlIadp_oEtDF8xVKd2x4tD4KvrhYLtM_hzsfxX16fG3wEAAP__OTKOgw), where poverty, persecution, and isolation often keep them from ever hearing the Good News. Through the **_No Child Forgotten_ Matching Challenge**, [_your donation's impact is _](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLvOnDAQRp_GdIuGsbkVLiLx022KNCmRsccwkS8IvLvat482yRukPdJ39On8vC93w4HT9t1E0sXnJdHrClQKnUukmE82wZl3ycdKxu4lv9JlFgRsb9DesFuc4fBeVOW0J0KSFemmVwO2Ckaodr0q66RvLPh2QBzX1fvRqxXAd11j-rFi_bFBi10DMMBY-75pJJCl1akVyAgF5Ljks_6VOZWdfHjnJ521zbEKei_luIT8JnAWOG_85LTVOdGeD6oTFYHzYTYSOGN7S9nuHJzP55ZLoRStkPOjxOXKj9OSkNP8Vy6w--BIjh9RyOkrGg7_oDXxMLwlIadp_oEtDF8xVKd2x4tD4KvrhYLtM_hzsfxX16fG3wEAAP__OTKOgw)[_**DOUBLED**_](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLvOnDAQRp_GdIuGsbkVLiLx022KNCmRsccwkS8IvLvat482yRukPdJ39On8vC93w4HT9t1E0sXnJdHrClQKnUukmE82wZl3ycdKxu4lv9JlFgRsb9DesFuc4fBeVOW0J0KSFemmVwO2Ckaodr0q66RvLPh2QBzX1fvRqxXAd11j-rFi_bFBi10DMMBY-75pJJCl1akVyAgF5Ljks_6VOZWdfHjnJ521zbEKei_luIT8JnAWOG_85LTVOdGeD6oTFYHzYTYSOGN7S9nuHJzP55ZLoRStkPOjxOXKj9OSkNP8Vy6w--BIjh9RyOkrGg7_oDXxMLwlIadp_oEtDF8xVKd2x4tD4KvrhYLtM_hzsfxX16fG3wEAAP__OTKOgw)[ --up to $500,000](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLvOnDAQRp_GdIuGsbkVLiLx022KNCmRsccwkS8IvLvat482yRukPdJ39On8vC93w4HT9t1E0sXnJdHrClQKnUukmE82wZl3ycdKxu4lv9JlFgRsb9DesFuc4fBeVOW0J0KSFemmVwO2Ckaodr0q66RvLPh2QBzX1fvRqxXAd11j-rFi_bFBi10DMMBY-75pJJCl1akVyAgF5Ljks_6VOZWdfHjnJ521zbEKei_luIT8JnAWOG_85LTVOdGeD6oTFYHzYTYSOGN7S9nuHJzP55ZLoRStkPOjxOXKj9OSkNP8Vy6w--BIjh9RyOkrGg7_oDXxMLwlIadp_oEtDF8xVKd2x4tD4KvrhYLtM_hzsfxX16fG3wEAAP__OTKOgw)--to help reach **3 million children** with God's Word in places like majority-Muslim and Buddhist regions, as well as children like Ivana, an Indigenous child in Peru. Now, Ivana is sharing God's Word in her community! But **millions more children are still waiting**. In remote villages. In dangerous regions. In spiritual darkness. Will you help ensure no child is forgotten? [**_Give before the deadline and double your impact. clean_body: The Memorial Day weather is likely to be split today, with cool, pleasant temperatures in the Midwest and Northeast and rain and possible in Southern states. | | | | | | Monday, May 26, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1927, Henry Ford and his son Edsel drove out of their factory, marking the iconic automobile's official final day of production. We hope you're having a restful Memorial Day as we reflect on those who served. One of the most powerful tributes this weekend is unfolding on the National Mall, where USAA's 134-foot returns to commemorate more than 600,000 fallen service members. Today's and tomorrow's editions are shorter so our team can spend time with loved ones. Thanks for reading--full coverage returns Wednesday! Today's sponsor is , a global ministry dedicated to sharing the Gospel with children in some of the world's hardest-to-reach places. | Memorial Day Severe Weather Possible The Memorial Day weather is likely to be split today, with cool, pleasant temperatures in the Midwest and Northeast and rain and possible in Southern states. Areas surrounding cities like Oklahoma City, Little Rock, Memphis, and Fort Worth are at the highest risk of severe weather today, with , severe storms, and tornadoes. Today will cap off a weekend of festivities across the country, with the National Memorial Day Parade in , being the largest event on the docket. Forecasts are calling for an overcast parade in D.C., which will include appearances from Kiss rock star Gene Simmons and four Medal of Honor recipients as the Grand Marshals. Military Spends Big to Rebuild Ranks The U.S. military has poured over over the last three years, scrambling to reverse enlistment declines worsened by the COVID-19 pandemic. The Navy, despite being smaller than the Army, led spending efforts, to both officers and enlisted sailors, with some incentives topping $150,000. The Army’s biggest comeback move was the launch of the . This course helps borderline candidates meet fitness and academic standards. It now boasts a 95% graduation rate and has sent nearly 25,000 recruits to basic training. Meanwhile, across all branches, pandemic-era setbacks, like school closures and rising youth disqualification rates, continue to shape a more expensive, more aggressive fight for manpower. Top Beach Towns to Visit or Buy In Realtor.com’s 2025 list of the for homebuyers ranks Pascagoula, Mississippi, at No. 1, with a median list price of $165,610. Other top entries include Deerfield Beach, Florida ($211,750), and ($310,925). If you’re not in the market to buy a beach property, just want to enjoy a perfect beach, list includes Coopers Beach in Southampton, New York, along with Beachwalker Park in South Carolina and Delnor-Wiggins Pass in Florida. Love reading The Flyover? mailto:?cc=editor@jointheflyover.com&subject=My%20new%20favorite%20newsletter!&body=Hey!%0D%0A%0D%0AI%E2%80%99ve%20been%20reading%20a%20new%20newsletter%20every%20morning%20called%20The%20Flyover.%20I%20love%20it!%20I%20think%20you%27ll%20love%20it%20too.%0D%0A%0D%0AIt%E2%80%99s%20my%20new%20favorite%20source%20for%20news%20first%20thing%20in%20the%20morning.%20They%20cover%20important%20and%20interesting%20stories%E2%80%94usually%20politics%2C%20sports%2C%20finance%2C%20science%20%26%20tech%2C%20etc.%0D%0A%0D%0AI%20think%20you%20should%20give%20it%20a%20try.%0D%0A%0D%0AJust%20copy%20this%20link%20and%20paste%20it%20into%20your%20browser%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fjointheflyover.com%2F%3Futm%3D83%0D%0A%0D%0ALet%20me%20know%20if%20you%20like%20it.%20I%20think%20you%20will!%0D%0A to share with your friends and family. | Flying together with our sponsor No Child Forgotten. No Heart Left Unreached. Right now, you have a limited-time opportunity to help , where poverty, persecution, and isolation often keep them from ever hearing the Good News. Through the No Child Forgotten Matching Challenge, help reach 3 million children with God's Word in places like majority-Muslim and Buddhist regions, as well as children like Ivana, an Indigenous child in Peru. When she saw the Lumo Project Films The Son of Man , she said: "I learned Jesus came to save us because He loves us ... I have decided to give Him my life." Now, Ivana is sharing God's Word in her community! But millions more children are still waiting. In remote villages. In dangerous regions. In spiritual darkness. | | Fellow Conservative, Today, I join millions of Americans in remembering and honoring the men and women who have died while serving our great Nation in the United States Armed Forces. I encourage you to do the same- _and to never forget the price of our freedom._ Thank you and God bless you,

Ted Cruz

name: Gary Sinise
subject: These heroes are counting on you
body:
It's Gary Sinise here...

It's Gary Sinise here... I'm grateful to again serve as an Honorary Grand Marshal on the 20th anniversary of this important event. It's Gary Sinise here... I'm grateful to again serve as an Honorary Grand Marshal on the 20th anniversary of this important event.
| | | [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~)
---
Friend, It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. **Now, I'm grateful to again serve as an Honorary Grand Marshal on the 20th anniversary of this important event.** This year will be a remembrance like no other, as we commemorate the 80th anniversary of victory in World War II and the 250th birthdays of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps. **[Friend, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENTIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous Americans like you. Will you help chip in today to help support the Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~)**
---
[ **Support the National Memorial Day Parade** ](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~)
---
[](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~)
---
The National Memorial Day Parade costs a fraction of other major public events to put on each year. In fact, _the entire parade_ costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! _That means that every dollar that 's contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event._ **But I want to reiterate that American Veterans Center does not take ANY federal funding for this event. It is funded _entirely_ by private citizens like you who want to honor those who have sacrificed so much for our country.** Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the Parade, but also supports great programs like:
* Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to Today before we lose these stories forever.

* Hold our National Youth Summit which allows young students a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, and Vietnam.

* Produce veteran focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom.

[**Please join me in support of the National Memorial Day Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, $250, $500, or more right now.**](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~) If you've been looking for an opportunity to give back to those who have given so much for our freedom, this is one of the best possible ways you could do it. _And for a special group of veterans, this might be one of the last chances we get._ You see, with each year that goes by, we lose more and more of our World War II veterans, and for the few we have remaining, traveling to Washington, D.C., for the parade is difficult. WWII veterans were a key part of the first parade when it was revived in 2005, as they have been every year since for the last 20 years. _But this may be the last year we have the opportunity to have these brave men and women with us for the parade._** ** **That's why we are making a concerted effort to ensure these veterans are at this year's National Memorial Day Parade to honor them one last time. ** So this year, as we mark the 80th anniversary of victory in Europe and Japan to end World War II - and the 250th anniversaries of the Army, Navy, and Marine Corps - I encourage you to help us honor these brave veterans while we still have them with us.
---
[ **Support the National Memorial Day Parade** ](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~)
---
And no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you. On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. _I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more._ _**[Friend, thank you for your support of America's heroes, and please - if you're able, make a gift to fund the National Memorial Day Parade right now during its 20th anniversary.](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~)**_ Sincerely, Gary Sinise Honorary Grand Marshal National Memorial Day Parade
---
[](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~)
---
[ **Support the National Memorial Day Parade** ](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/YOgMRlmjwrHOCOnu63yRKg~~/AARclBA~/ZXeRRioYHM5BqO5Husf5-c1Nk_PoA0ao8L4ah4BC7HKWFfcDIYuX_l-8K1SWvqnbHprVA8oQLdUg_EBVkqC7LdpYZqYnuwNHrE12dDf64-rHzgXy5wMcQxMtjclUGkVFOWcGxa9l8YmeJ7Bjvq4PMO9nqzUa-dy1vCiViyheV1dbDXC9tYn5FC4srf6shSkUaKGVGbZ72PZP_IRGAhfxSG3D4w_1maQapB-BI7GmxsY~)
---
The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center
1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201
---

Pop Acta Media | Conservative Broadcast
---
_PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/eFHadoCN5m_-ghNn5Jas-g~~/AARclBA~/o9Iahi0HrTRR5JPl9VyUDs1I9KSuKuqrZxnafPv4t9zzNsT8Oj-qlcXgP3n6B3QW7oN8fk6GJ-INVLvh8fEHlL56sD5VV_FjLSXLx5eqamjOM2SJd4kHHGqC0T-4_-E_8kBL4RrUJgG1Jdcy79eXxbQQLzCDxJ5hN9iwVTTqypMHxOnBlODrU_sRHBzl13_Q)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/b3AFBppa1UdQ6dDedThwuQ~~/AARclBA~/t2_x8o8BmNhKU6JDQvK-75CWc8CkgKIrpJAku4BqmscLdwc00fUWl2_h0amOVQGjS6oKCkXIPvNKF5brFvVHif3to7oUMfZVv4wFNdzl_zZmF3cluGE1i8pvIrE9gQuxG3DbiStV8OkArE_FMMXlVA~~)_

name: The Texas Flyover
subject: Ten Commandments Bill, Hot Air Balloon Race, and Dinos in the Wild
body:
The Texas House approved a bill that would require public schools to [display a copy of the Ten Commandments](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQRp8GOiN7gm0oXGxDl223RP4ZgiVjIzwbkre_IlX62410pKM537_7fLcxxfz4azc09FrKnPGsCYnwmAmzL9tmc9gwU3UxpbWQjccMHCTjkoGag43pPfdtMI6HUbRohO4HkPrGx3Y16JSUYfSo0WkYwPWDXqzTg_QK7GjbaC4Zl6CE4KJX3QAghJfcj2FBCX3TcwyRytHRioQvW5f0Lk88Ol-2NpmVaK_N7U8DUwPTeZ5ddv6quHgD015SpOjr18ku3MD0sbGrixFm9h3L9v8uRc-qX0tJlR0-W-BqkKI9TNjPmFKsSjc9f2w2ps8v9LsFnwZ-AgAA___uFogG) in each classroom. Senate Bill 10 passed 82 to 46 yesterday, with three Democrats joining Republicans to advance the measure.

| | |
---
| [](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u3CAQhZ8Gd7YAA_YWFJGutrtpU6KxGdsj8WMBu_fu20feRHmBtOd8mjn6fn26T6BAaf8JEW373rJL-FUDtobFNUxrjhGSj5haXSiEIzeg4iSXuue6l8Z5oPByqvN24f4mOrRiUrPU08hv3WFh4VpufJ6Bg_d-nb0AjbgYWLyazdiRvY5xLY0QXCgzzFIKsWq-3vyGWiqmOHpquQztwIbfULfwyk8sw5pjF-zR2lnZ-IPJO5N3Sk-sbQi4U07xQq58vD9adDU_yops_MAIFJg0VxjR0yOy8eME8v0JpSUs9aDzb79CPIH29Cb-lWLmveKT--NAaDNxqY0SY1esP78oBKpmYorv16v30vZ_fp9W_g4AAP__ZsGPug) | |
# Monday, May 26, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1993, Texas Rangers outfielder Jose Canseco was immortalized for one of the most infamous bloopers in baseball history when a fly ball bounced off his head and over the wall for a home run. ([Watch Video](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0M1qMyEUxvGr0V0GPep8LFy8vCVQSgpZlC7F0ZOMoGOYOckkd1-mt9Dt88Bv8f8-uZNPOc3XT1_Q0vNS3YzbmpEIF0c4h1qKn2PBmdYx5TxV8mlxIMAchDlA66JP-eU0j3YUcZAcrex0D6ZTYuCTVXrohQIf-yCD6tD4bmiVGpUa-6CN4cnumDDQSimkbpseQMpgRBjiBQ1opgXGRHVpaELCp18v-VUfuDShFp7tRHRbmfrH4MjguG1b86p3uo-4__viKUxMHR9MvZ3T83z6uuv_H-98sfG2pZzT2nZMi2vxKf-S9LcQDws_AQAA__9j4XEm)) We honor our nation's heroes this Memorial Day. Today and tomorrow, we are bringing you abbreviated editions to give us time to be with our families. We will return with a full edition on Wednesday. We're sponsored today by [Legion M](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u3CAQhZ8Gd7YAA_YWFJGutrtpU6KxGdsj8WMBu_fu20feRHmBtOd8mjn6fn26T6BAaf8JEW373rJL-FUDtobFNUxrjhGSj5haXSiEIzeg4iSXuue6l8Z5oPByqvN24f4mOrRiUrPU08hv3WFh4VpufJ6Bg_d-nb0AjbgYWLyazdiRvY5xLY0QXCgzzFIKsWq-3vyGWiqmOHpquQztwIbfULfwyk8sw5pjF-zR2lnZ-IPJO5N3Sk-sbQi4U07xQq58vD9adDU_yops_MAIFJg0VxjR0yOy8eME8v0JpSUs9aDzb79CPIH29Cb-lWLmveKT--NAaDNxqY0SY1esP78oBKpmYorv16v30vZ_fp9W_g4AAP__ZsGPug), offering an investment opportunity for all movie lovers.

## Ten Commandments Bill Passes House
The Texas House approved a bill that would require public schools to [display a copy of the Ten Commandments](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQRp8GOiN7gm0oXGxDl223RP4ZgiVjIzwbkre_IlX62410pKM537_7fLcxxfz4azc09FrKnPGsCYnwmAmzL9tmc9gwU3UxpbWQjccMHCTjkoGag43pPfdtMI6HUbRohO4HkPrGx3Y16JSUYfSo0WkYwPWDXqzTg_QK7GjbaC4Zl6CE4KJX3QAghJfcj2FBCX3TcwyRytHRioQvW5f0Lk88Ol-2NpmVaK_N7U8DUwPTeZ5ddv6quHgD015SpOjr18ku3MD0sbGrixFm9h3L9v8uRc-qX0tJlR0-W-BqkKI9TNjPmFKsSjc9f2w2ps8v9LsFnwZ-AgAA___uFogG) in each classroom. Senate Bill 10 passed 82 to 46 yesterday, with three Democrats joining Republicans to advance the measure. State Rep. Candy Noble (R-Lucas) noted that the Ten Commandments had been taught in the nation’s schools prior to a since-abandoned [1971 Supreme Court decision](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L9ugzAQgPGnMRvIPmwgg4cuLG26dkQGH-Ek_4nMFZI-fZW-QvdPv-H7uk5XR4HS7dNFtPxY85Tw3AMyY5kY05JjdMlHTLzPFMKW2VGZQIKppamhm7yj8Jx05e0s_UVVaFWvBzB9Ky_VZt3catMbkOilBze3q1IDyn4Aib3pVEX2hUkDnVJS6a4ZAJRajFwufkUDWmiJnjiXhjdkfLh9Dc98YGmWHKtgN-b7Lto3AaOA8TzPZi7ELiVa3CsRMHK-0yJg_MCYU33U79-Ff6JLVbH-flIItHe90PIWHYU_lv8347DwGwAA__-4lnPG) that stipulated a law’s primary effect must not advance religion. In the hours-long debate, Democrats tried to add amendments that would have allowed local school boards to block the mandate, required the Ten Commandments to be [displayed alongside](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGS2zAMRL9G6uAhcaRsFyzSqLu0KTUQCZ04oUiHxEn232d0TdJfC8zu7Hu_3qd3iinmj5-0sZPnUqbMR0sswnUSzr5sG-WwcZY2x5TWIhTrhAotKAs4TIFiek2mD25W4a57dvpqbmivb-rer24JM9Ltbr33VyTGm7eDmhdjtfKG2fTRnWXK4qC10ma43BC19lb5e1jYoumM4hCl1IusLPyktqRX2blefNn65FaRR-vefnQ4djgex3H5_aR8PjscT5oOx68YPEqKEv2_w1o-GwOFnbLnBicgSIHKfz5jZRDO8L8AiBl45_oCn6i1WsoGtAhX2IswlAyyMjSaZ5K1w7GvLjyOmFJsw7Uz6mOjmL5Wy_dc7w7_BgAA__8rPJhc) the major tenets of other religions, and removed references to “manservant” and “maidservant.” On Friday, the House approved legislation allowing—but not requiring—school districts to designate a time during the school day for employees and students to [pray and read the Bible](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D3u4yAUBPDT4M4WPIOxC4pt3GXbLREfjxgJ7Cx-Gye3XyVX-Pe_mZHmz83eXC55v_92FQ290mF3vM6CRNgs4R6OWt0eK-50-lzKdpDLzQIH1XPVw2Sjy-VtZReN53ERHRqh5QxKj3zpNpPSorxPMI9Sx0kr4aKMbpQBceZhWbpsPmVcwSQEF3IaZgAhguJhiQkVSCY5xkxHG2hDwpc7U3kfT2xDOGpXzEb0ONn4i8HKYL2u6-MC7oTt_PvPNfw4Bus3ymB1jXIoaNELqaeR9yEG30sVdO_nNPbexcCjVABuHjaqpWsmPq5cSj4nzSS_V5fLd5x-dtnTwP8AAAD__9ZZf9s). Parents must consent to their children participating
## Great Texas Balloon Race Coming Up
The National Hot Air Balloon Championship and the [Great Texas Balloon Race](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykzzuO7CAQQNHVQNZWUQa7OyB4ibN-6YQITNlG4tPCpf7sfjSzhVnAvdL5uru7TznV_b8vZPm9NVfpdWZipu6Y6tpK8TUWqnyGlPPR2KfuENBcwFxwctGn_HFaRhsg3pQkq2Z9RTOPcJOHBaVns_k16nHEKWxBQTDhOs6kI6oIMtmfGRiclAKlp-GKqNRqYL3FjQxqoYFi4tYHPojp7c8tf9qT-rC2IrM9mB-nGP8JXAQuO4c-VGKBi-w2Pl4p53ROs9CwF5_yb8R_oz4tfgcAAP__LSxnRw) are coming to Longview next month, bringing over six dozen balloons and more than 100,000 spectators to the region. Established in 1978, the Great Texas Balloon Race is one of the largest hot air balloon events in the country. In addition to a race, the event includes a balloon glow, tethered rides, [concerts](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ_GdEb2ggkULq6hy7VXImMvwcpiR2YPkrf_RV7hr0ea-b75d5_uLlJMj79uQ8vvJU8Jz52QGcvEmHzeNpfChon3ORKtmV0sEygwUhkJ3RRcpM_UVsHOKgy6QqtvbQ_m1qihWq3pzayCaTxC26P3Q9d3oVPaNLoBRKiivcqUgU5rpduu7gG09kb5ISxooBWtwhA5l5pXZHy7faFPPrDUPm8V2ZX5tYvmj4BRwHieZ_1Efl6hgPHi_58ifwSMK7pAMWGReFw-suCBjjDIJRf5VXoUdCy_I3J2RDknWZxHAWNVbHidkSju3U206rG5SF8E_t1xh4WfAAAA___9E4Nk), and a drone show. For the [third consecutive year](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykz7uOqzAQgOGnMR3IM9g4KVychi6n3RIZewBLvkRmNpe3X2VfYdu_-KT_67bcXEyx7P9dJsuvrS6FnmciZmoLU_E1Z1dCpsLnGlM6KrvYFpSoe6l7nJbgYnovqgt2leEKHVkw6oLajPLaHVaTugCQn7ZtVBrBrMaApHH04OREpov2g0mNE4AENQ0XRACvpb-GjTQqoSSFyLUNfBDTy51betcHtcHX3CV7MN9PMf4TOAucd17bUIgFzm6t39zzQf0nCpy7ZsP9GVOK52SEknt2Mf0q_Lf3h8WfAAAA___2qGz5), Longview is also hosting the National Hot Air Balloon Championship. The championship is June 10-15, with the Great Texas Balloon Race taking place June 13–15. Event founder Bill Bussey, an 82-year-old retired dentist, is one of the pilots planning to compete. "_I’m the oldest one in the competition by far—by about 12 years,_ " [he said](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOrCAQBeCnwQ4DKKgFxW3s9ra3NCDjSu4IG5hdd9_-j_sKfzPFnOTLyfl3W24uYkz3v-4AS-8tLwnOikAEZSFIaz4Ol8IBiaqPiHsmF8uihNJcaK7MElzEz9I3wXoRJtmAlUM_Kj10Ymp26wYvjPad6WQnjdfjCELLvhs6A4P3von2woRWRkohe9OOSkm5arFOYQOtetYLCJFyaWkHgrerG37yC0q75qNBuxM9Kuv-MDUzNZ_n2f5Hel0hU_NlMzWL68iJqflewBH_Mtw7xJwTL24FvuVnClB4gQ1hpfr9Vr7HSrl8eN1dgcof6FLl25OeBZiam2LD44yIsZqB9eJ-uIjfXvS7NV9W_QQAAP__pjaJlA).

By [turning fans into shareholders](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u3CAQhZ8Gd7YAA_YWFJGutrtpU6KxGdsj8WMBu_fu20feRHmBtOd8mjn6fn26T6BAaf8JEW373rJL-FUDtobFNUxrjhGSj5haXSiEIzeg4iSXuue6l8Z5oPByqvN24f4mOrRiUrPU08hv3WFh4VpufJ6Bg_d-nb0AjbgYWLyazdiRvY5xLY0QXCgzzFIKsWq-3vyGWiqmOHpquQztwIbfULfwyk8sw5pjF-zR2lnZ-IPJO5N3Sk-sbQi4U07xQq58vD9adDU_yops_MAIFJg0VxjR0yOy8eME8v0JpSUs9aDzb79CPIH29Cb-lWLmveKT--NAaDNxqY0SY1esP78oBKpmYorv16v30vZ_fp9W_g4AAP__ZsGPug), the studio gains a built-in audience before a project is even released. The goal? Harness the power of the audience to change the game. And they’re already doing it. 2025 is their highest-revenue year yet, thanks to a seven-figure international deal with Universal. It brings their total revenue up to $7M+. Their latest film, _My Dead Friend Zoe_ (starring Sonequa Martin-Green, Natalie Morales, Morgan Freeman, and Ed Harris), received a **95% Rotten Tomatoes score** , making it one of the highest-rated movies of Morgan Freeman’s career (even higher than _The Shawshank Redemption_!). It happened because **54,000+ movie-loving investors** had a say in the process. Senate Bill 10 passed 82 to 46 yesterday, with three Democrats joining Republicans to advance the measure. State Rep. Candy Noble (R-Lucas) noted that the Ten Commandments had been taught in the nation’s schools prior to a since-abandoned that stipulated a law’s primary effect must not advance religion. In the hours-long debate, Democrats tried to add amendments that would have allowed local school boards to block the mandate, required the Ten Commandments to be the major tenets of other religions, and removed references to “manservant” and “maidservant.” On Friday, the House approved legislation allowing—but not requiring—school districts to designate a time during the school day for employees and students to . Parents must consent to their children participating Great Texas Balloon Race Coming Up The National Hot Air Balloon Championship and the are coming to Longview next month, bringing over six dozen balloons and more than 100,000 spectators to the region. Established in 1978, the Great Texas Balloon Race is one of the largest hot air balloon events in the country. In addition to a race, the event includes a balloon glow, tethered rides, , and a drone show. For the , Longview is also hosting the National Hot Air Balloon Championship. The championship is June 10-15, with the Great Texas Balloon Race taking place June 13–15. Event founder Bill Bussey, an 82-year-old retired dentist, is one of the pilots planning to compete. When I saw Alexi Whitney the second time, nearly forty years after the first, I was stunned. My vague memory of meeting him when we were both small children is that he was a wild child. But at that second meeting, my second cousin was wholly transformed. He’d served in Afghanistan and Iraq as a [...]

Ararat High School is losing its athletics director at the end of the school-year, according to a resignation letter submitted on Thursday, just days after the school revealed a police investigation into a different, now former, teacher. ------------------------------------------------------------

name: Colonel Pam Stevenson
subject: I’m a veteran running for U.S. Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
| | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta50223d9-3506-4f6f-9fc3-1e0aa7e0f03c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4991b39a-51f1-48cf-b103-e016cbbf1bc0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Peter, I’m Col. Pam Stevenson and I’m running to represent Kentucky in the U.S. Senate. For too long, I’ve watched my state’s Senator, Mitch McConnell, and his GOP allies put special interests over our state, country, and Constitution. It’s unacceptable. As a veteran, I took an oath to defend our Constitution and the American people against any threat. I refuse to sit back and let Republicans continue to attack our access to food, healthcare, education, and other necessities we depend on. [As Kentucky’s US Senator, I will fight to protect our rights and freedoms, Peter. Will you help me build the grassroots power I’ll need to win by splitting a contribution between my campaign and Blue Amp Action today?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T04426cf9-07c1-435d-a629-4576f4e4e01b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [DONATE NOW →](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5ca2aef2-0a50-40da-bc7d-df9c48f10848/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| Peter, from representing the U.S. Air Force as a JAG attorney to founding a legal aid nonprofit for veterans to representing my community in the Kentucky State House, I have a long legacy of service to my state and country. [Now, I am ready to fight for Kentuckians – and all Americans – in the U.S. Senate, but I’ll need your help to get my campaign off to a strong start. Will you join my team by making a donation of $10 or more today to my campaign, Peter?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T11ae67da-5dfc-407f-b21f-44171eb37a6c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T72a8e8ea-ca0e-44a4-afe8-19b7aead6d98/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

subject: Our culture has a sick hatred for integrity and achievement
body:
I learned firsthand how our culture despises achievement and wishes to destroy anyone willing to challenge the status quo

|
---
| | [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/cPer4FXDoPfmA35PRnxMwA~~/AARclBA~/GZeW7QNwfAUP88AwzK6NoZyfE3K6YhVFv8DveHZTAXmEiSrOgjL4jnjQ2HzDM-ZXUV4apnrWDljEQcWnHSeVdeSFjmhmuDd3qC5QDDw8iJOWWABHPvMcptJtMewdMg97IyHQRbgz3MxBAaIsKjbzbZFTHpNuicl2e9q9HQIhMVfUA9pD22mYJYIQW1E0Gwv4lxmHRdYUApJAsgxc0r-1CA~~) Friend,

I learned firsthand how our culture despises achievement and wishes to destroy anyone willing to challenge the status quo, big government, or the Fake News media machine.

When I was ousted from Project Veritas, two big things (one you haven't heard about) happened to me.

FIRST: The media launched a vicious smear campaign against me like I've never seen before. **Make no mistake:** _They identified this as an opportunity to "cancel" me forever_ and tried everything in their power to destroy me and everything I've ever stood for.

They failed.

SECOND: I received an OVERWHELMING amount of support from patriots across the country. These brave men and women defended me, but more importantly, they stood up for the principles of liberty and free speech.

**One of my top supporters was The Atlas Society.**

Jennifer Grossman, the CEO of The Atlas Society, proudly stood by my side and even invited me to speak with a gathering of the organization's top student leaders about free speech and how Howard Roark, the fictional hero of _The Fountainhead_ by Ayn Rand, remains my inspiration to act with integrity.

Which is why I am personally reaching out to you.

I told Jennifer I would help The Atlas Society raise $25,000 in the next 48 hours by personally contacting my online supporters. [Please consider making an URGENT donation of any amount to The Atlas Society by clicking here.](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/cPer4FXDoPfmA35PRnxMwA~~/AARclBA~/GZeW7QNwfAUP88AwzK6NoZyfE3K6YhVFv8DveHZTAXmEiSrOgjL4jnjQ2HzDM-ZXUV4apnrWDljEQcWnHSeVdeSFjmhmuDd3qC5QDDw8iJOWWABHPvMcptJtMewdMg97IyHQRbgz3MxBAaIsKjbzbZFTHpNuicl2e9q9HQIhMVfUA9pD22mYJYIQW1E0Gwv4lxmHRdYUApJAsgxc0r-1CA~~)
[Donate >>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/cPer4FXDoPfmA35PRnxMwA~~/AARclBA~/GZeW7QNwfAUP88AwzK6NoZyfE3K6YhVFv8DveHZTAXmEiSrOgjL4jnjQ2HzDM-ZXUV4apnrWDljEQcWnHSeVdeSFjmhmuDd3qC5QDDw8iJOWWABHPvMcptJtMewdMg97IyHQRbgz3MxBAaIsKjbzbZFTHpNuicl2e9q9HQIhMVfUA9pD22mYJYIQW1E0Gwv4lxmHRdYUApJAsgxc0r-1CA~~)
Let me tell you more about The Atlas Society and how they are fundamentally changing the direction of our country.

_I will also tell you HOW The Atlas Society is spreading its message in what I consider one of the most innovative methods I 've EVER seen a nonprofit organization engage in before._

_**_So keep reading_!**_

My mentor, Andrew Breitbart, reminds us that politics is downstream from culture. But what is culture downstream from? Answer: Philosophy. That's where The Atlas Society shines, engaging young people with the virtues of reason, achievement, and benevolent self-interest in wildly creative, artistic ways.

Have you ever read Ayn Rand's _Atlas Shrugged_ or _The Fountainhead_?

These books changed how I saw the world and my role in it.

But they are 700+ pages long!

**Jennifer shared this terrifying statistic with me:** Daily reading of books among young people has fallen from 70% in the late 1970s to 12% today.

So, how do we preserve Rand's role as the greatest all-time recruiter for liberty? That's where The Atlas Society gets creative! They leverage art and technology to create programming for "_skimmers, swimmers, and deep divers_."
**Skimmers:** Social media memes and animated videos -- every single one of their 50 posts a week gets 40K comments, shares, and likes. The "Draw My Life" videos on concepts like Capitalism, Property Rights, and Free Speech reach MILLIONS of viewers. Links invite young people on a journey for deeper intellectual exploration.

**Swimmers:** Graphic novel adaptations of Ayn Rand's shorter books, Pocket Guides that distill big concepts like Postmodernism and Socialism into a 90-minute read. Weekly podcasts with top entrepreneurs and thought-leaders, and discussion groups like "Morals & Markets."

**Deep Divers:** They recruited an all-star academic faculty of philosophers and economists who guide students on a deeper study of Rand's philosophy, publish independent work, and provide programming at their summer conference for students (the Galt's Gulch Summit, where I spoke!).
Jennifer and The Atlas Society are accomplishing great things for our country, and I hope you will be one of the FIRST supporters of mine to make a donation and help me reach our $25,000 goal. Please use one of these links to donate:
[Donate $10 >>>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/3uhnkV7ZqmBH7k3w8Zozmw~~/AARclBA~/d84qj4e-CT8P-iJigTgFfXzabkgVoMGyBFGsibOOQsxdVUCUMTxAZdaKyS2qkDrbZQU4zgOGwSiGbLVqZHIPUK6y1G__8zUFDgTsWMghr-OSEYLl090Xgx3hHE-uXCCSmm5h0Aq6rvxFoy-hvk_Z0oRS9Mp3aHzh0qvVe-VOZFDPLoVrUAGD9W3cu_8Ou4KPtj-ryScV89_bai4PB1xNwQ~~)

[Donate $50 >>>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/BJfucYcDwDGFtrL85pG4jQ~~/AARclBA~/d84qj4e-CT8P-iJigTgFfXzabkgVoMGyBFGsibOOQsxdVUCUMTxAZdaKyS2qkDrbZQU4zgOGwSiGbLVqZHIPUK6y1G__8zUFDgTsWMghr-OSEYLl090Xgx3hHE-uXCCSZwQczc2SYDinFRTA-JodXyyC1XuBm2WeFYDsVA7Jb5o4PJSjz2b2Fj9dgFAnyoo8At28fv8SY5a-yGin9bPwxA~~)

[Donate $100 >>>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/RKTVjj_TaBwiyGeWlO3pFQ~~/AARclBA~/qfQajlA1GOyC6O_C3swzgQtnNxUrX3njcE3a4AGPDHvRTvS5tFWg5S7k7QIr7Ow7d_pVZr4_HYic86TKx4otSMSHyMmDnL_pPX7KXYMu9_Hjp0ZDMGbXBzKoy_eoHAVZ2-6i64GTzbQ31CkF3Nt6oyoNq8ohK2GrSL9kqPFUNbxBiKng_lzUwYSr18HjMBNCOvUbIsbmUQvg-XYOo81jZQ~~)

[Donate $250 >>>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/QF_2AnbT4qSj1AXzGQEu2Q~~/AARclBA~/qfQajlA1GOyC6O_C3swzgQtnNxUrX3njcE3a4AGPDHvRTvS5tFWg5S7k7QIr7Ow7d_pVZr4_HYic86TKx4otSMSHyMmDnL_pPX7KXYMu9_Hjp0ZDMGbXBzKoy_eoHAVZ1dkpcET2MIJqREqp5OraWwRIAn1kvbq7OwhHibCZaD8f7j1mTKrFoXfIZidBvx5Z6P7ACk0A5b9A82WE_EMojQ~~)

[Donate $500 >>>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/Dt_WWY1INd2s1RLvt4SuyA~~/AARclBA~/qfQajlA1GOyC6O_C3swzgQtnNxUrX3njcE3a4AGPDHvRTvS5tFWg5S7k7QIr7Ow7d_pVZr4_HYic86TKx4otSMSHyMmDnL_pPX7KXYMu9_Hjp0ZDMGbXBzKoy_eoHAVZINoO0XdkUkcX2HI_a16X4UFDZ5eYJbrWn8hMTnx_XEz2xc0l8AFRt09ukJ8KH0g2qJW0ENhihKmTIASXWjAkvA~~)

[Donate $1,000 >>>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/P3XoZST1xK4wHEzse7WW6A~~/AARclBA~/zmSl2VDuCYKXPAAfewSpzFbqgCRwh6pjEe4u2MzTCbuE1JkwYDx5qL_YiEaZd8t5b-htsEwxxo4CM8oO2dXTiULbZLPzlYgMRH0GVjT_zT4DOkZEOwArO1vpSaKbVff8YzfFsGtSZ0R65atE8_Xnho0nb2ipGJLrOpcuCMecaJWPjWrzUirouZhmOZwmtXeGy3I-c42frznwkrlIufH59g~~)

[Donate Any Amount >>>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/cPer4FXDoPfmA35PRnxMwA~~/AARclBA~/GZeW7QNwfAUP88AwzK6NoZyfE3K6YhVFv8DveHZTAXmEiSrOgjL4jnjQ2HzDM-ZXUV4apnrWDljEQcWnHSeVdeSFjmhmuDd3qC5QDDw8iJOWWABHPvMcptJtMewdMg97IyHQRbgz3MxBAaIsKjbzbZFTHpNuicl2e9q9HQIhMVfUA9pD22mYJYIQW1E0Gwv4lxmHRdYUApJAsgxc0r-1CA~~)
Ultimately, here is what The Atlas Society is fighting FOR:
* Free market capitalism
* Rational self-interest
* Respect for achievement and success
* Free speech
* The future of young people and our great country

And here is what The Atlas Society is fighting AGAINST:
* Cancel Culture
* Our Educational system indoctrinating young people to embrace socialism
* Our sick culture that demonizes success and embraces twisted ideas
* Big government

I am inviting you to support The Atlas Society with a donation of ANY amount.

As a personal favor to me, please make your donation within the next 48 hours so I can help Jennifer reach our goal of $25,000.

[Here is the link to donate one more time.](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/cPer4FXDoPfmA35PRnxMwA~~/AARclBA~/GZeW7QNwfAUP88AwzK6NoZyfE3K6YhVFv8DveHZTAXmEiSrOgjL4jnjQ2HzDM-ZXUV4apnrWDljEQcWnHSeVdeSFjmhmuDd3qC5QDDw8iJOWWABHPvMcptJtMewdMg97IyHQRbgz3MxBAaIsKjbzbZFTHpNuicl2e9q9HQIhMVfUA9pD22mYJYIQW1E0Gwv4lxmHRdYUApJAsgxc0r-1CA~~)

Thank you for everything,

James O'Keefe

P.S. Here is a picture of me speaking with The Atlas Society's top student leaders.
[](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/cPer4FXDoPfmA35PRnxMwA~~/AARclBA~/GZeW7QNwfAUP88AwzK6NoZyfE3K6YhVFv8DveHZTAXmEiSrOgjL4jnjQ2HzDM-ZXUV4apnrWDljEQcWnHSeVdeSFjmhmuDd3qC5QDDw8iJOWWABHPvMcptJtMewdMg97IyHQRbgz3MxBAaIsKjbzbZFTHpNuicl2e9q9HQIhMVfUA9pD22mYJYIQW1E0Gwv4lxmHRdYUApJAsgxc0r-1CA~~) These students inspire me. Sadly, young people are learning to embrace socialism, reject critical thinking skills, and shed their creativity.

Well, NOT these students.

The Atlas Society is teaching students that they CAN accomplish great things, become leaders, and inspire others to do the same.

[Please take action by making a donation to The Atlas Society today. You can click here to donate $10, $50, $100, $500, $1,000, or ANY amount.](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/cPer4FXDoPfmA35PRnxMwA~~/AARclBA~/GZeW7QNwfAUP88AwzK6NoZyfE3K6YhVFv8DveHZTAXmEiSrOgjL4jnjQ2HzDM-ZXUV4apnrWDljEQcWnHSeVdeSFjmhmuDd3qC5QDDw8iJOWWABHPvMcptJtMewdMg97IyHQRbgz3MxBAaIsKjbzbZFTHpNuicl2e9q9HQIhMVfUA9pD22mYJYIQW1E0Gwv4lxmHRdYUApJAsgxc0r-1CA~~)

Thank you,

James

_The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible._

#### RCV Field Manual App Your guide to Ranked Choice Voting—anytime, anywhere.
#### Based on Field Manual to Ranked Choice Voting: Lessons from the Last Frontier, this app delivers clear, evidence-based insights into RCV’s impact on U.S. elections.
#### From Maine to San Francisco, explore real-world case studies, funding sources, and implementation challenges—all from your phone or computer. Features: Chapter-based navigation Searchable case studies and data Expert analysis and critiques Offline and mobile access Stay informed. Understand RCV. Share the truth.
---
---
| [](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=683453b855cb3777e21ca452&u=https%3A%2F%2Fizon-book-manufactured-majority-viewer-23332009477.us-west1.run.app%23%2Fpage%2Fchapter8&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_6834522c805d2942116e6a9a&l=en-US&s=amwOpQcPvVmH73VGo5YnxqMaYiE%3D)

#### Manufactured Majority App Alaska’s 2022 Elections—Unpacked. Anywhere. Anytime.
#### Based on Manufactured Majority by Phil Izon, this app gives you instant access to a deep dive into Alaska’s 2022 U.S. House and Senate races under ranked-choice voting and jungle primaries.
#### From skyrocketing costs to record-low turnout, explore the real consequences behind the headlines. Key Features: Election data and turnout insights Breakdown of vote-splitting and outcomes Mobile-friendly analysis of RCV impacts Access from any phone or computer Understand what really happened—and why it matters.
---
---
| [](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=683453b855cb3777e21ca452&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tropictundra.com%2Fthe-permanent-short&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_6834522c805d2942116e6a9a&l=en-US&s=znXfECLRusbvIwsfbqXnwNkZpB0%3D)
---
#### This Memorial Day, while we honor the service and sacrifice of those who gave everything for our freedoms, we also reflect on what those freedoms are meant to protect—**honest government, fair opportunity, and a voice for the people**.
#### But in Juneau, the people are being forgotten.
#### The Legislature just closed its latest session—not with discipline, not with restraint, but with a **stampede of giveaways**. Millions of dollars in taxpayer funds handed over to the **National Education Association** and entrenched union bureaucracies—while schools close their doors, and more Alaskan families than ever turn to charter schools and homeschooling.
#### What was the dividend meant to do?
The **Permanent Fund Dividend** was designed as a promise—that all Alaskans would share in the wealth of our natural resources. It was never meant to become a **slush fund for lobbyists, consultants, and political machines controlled by out-of-state interests.**
#### This is not just bad budgeting.
It’s a betrayal of the people—and the purpose—of Alaska.
#### Our upcoming film, _The Permanent Short: Alaska’s Billion Dollar Betrayal_ , will expose this corruption. We’ll follow the money and show how generations of Alaskans are being denied their share, their voice, and their future.
#### And the movement is already growing.
#### Our **Angel tickets were the first to sell out for “Frozen Odds”** —a powerful signal that Alaskans are ready to fight back. Angel supporters receive exclusive access to both _The Permanent Short_ and _Frozen Odds_ , the film that lit the fuse.
#### This is just the beginning.
#### The fight for Alaska’s future isn’t over.
It’s just getting started.
---
#### [**Click Here To Get Your Angel Ticket**](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=683453b855cb3777e21ca452&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tropictundra.com%2Fthe-permanent-short&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_6834522c805d2942116e6a9a&l=en-US&s=znXfECLRusbvIwsfbqXnwNkZpB0%3D)
---
#### **In 2001, Enron collapsed under the weight of one of the largest corporate frauds in American history—over $25 billion evaporated overnight, leaving pensions gutted and lives destroyed. It was called the smartest disaster money could buy.**
#### But what if the next Enron wasn’t a corporation?
What if it was your own government?
#### This film asks the billion-dollar question:
**What happens when a state quietly reallocates tens of billions of dollars meant for its citizens—without a vote, without consent, and without accountability?**
#### We follow the money—through shadowy nonprofits, bloated bureaucracies, and politically connected networks like **Arabella Advisors** , **Alaska Venture Fund** , and the **1630 Fund**.
#### It’s a financial betrayal not buried in spreadsheets—but broadcast in silence.
**$25 billion gone** —not from shareholders this time, but from the people of Alaska.
#### This is _The Permanent Short_.
#### More announcements coming soon.
**Thank you for being part of this journey.**
---
[_ _ Click Here To Get Your Angel Ticket _ _](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=683453b855cb3777e21ca452&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tropictundra.com%2Fthe-permanent-short&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_6834522c805d2942116e6a9a&l=en-US&s=znXfECLRusbvIwsfbqXnwNkZpB0%3D)
---
|
---
### **Now we have 108 supporters for “Frozen Odds”**
---
#### **Frozen Odds How do you take on $15 million with nothing but determination, grassroots organizing, and a clipboard? Frozen Odds is a gripping documentary that uncovers the raw power of citizen-led initiatives in Alaska. Following the journey of a small but determined movement, this film reveals how everyday people—outspent 100 to 1 by out-of-state interests—rallied together to challenge the system. Led by an Alaskans for Alaskans who helped write the law and push it to the ballot, this battle wasn’t just about policy—it was about democracy in its purest form. With 400+ volunteers braving the cold, gathering 42,000 signatures when only 26,000 were needed, they proved that people—not money—should shape the laws that govern them. Through intimate interviews, on-the-ground footage, and stunning Alaskan landscapes, Frozen Odds captures the high-stakes struggle against political elites (aka Lisa Murkowski and George Soros, etc.) and the enduring spirit of grassroots activism. Though the campaign fell just 700 votes short out of 320,000, it laid the groundwork for a new wave of initiatives—this time, with three fresh petitions ready to take the fight forward. In a world where billionaires and lobbyists try to control the political process.**
#### **Frozen Odds asks a bold question: What happens when ordinary citizens refuse to back down?**
---
---

| | | | Friends,

Below is a link to the CBS Face the Nation segment produced for Memorial Day which features an interview with For Country Caucus Members at the Vietnam Wall. Margaret Brennan also gave an interview to Bloomberg about the segment Friday where she reflected: "We are in a really bad place (with loss of trust in institutions and polarization). And that's why this Memorial Day weekend I did the shoot with the For Country Caucus. Because it's so rare these days that we find people like the For Country Caucus who will say we want to put policy over politics." A link to the interview is [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=e032d0787c&e=33f53a5b1a).

Also posted below are reflections by two of the remaining three Vietnam veterans in Congress before and after we cleaned the Wall together.

God bless our sacred war dead and their families.

Hello, I hope that you're enjoying this weekend with friends and family.
Memorial Day weekend is the unofficial beginning of Summer. Summer 2025 is here! How does it feel?
I grew up in upstate New York and then a suburb of New York City in Westchester County. There were not many kids there from military families. A couple guys from my high school went to West Point or the Air Force Academy but they were somewhat unusual. Growing up, Memorial Day weekend simply meant barbecues and the beginning of summer movie season.
This is not to say I wasn't patriotic - I've loved America my entire life. Perhaps that's the norm among children of immigrants. As a kid, I used to get into playful fights with my cousins because I would always say America was the best country on Earth. I founded an organization called Venture for America that I ran for six years. And of course, I ran for President because I thought I could help improve things for people.
It was only after I ran for President that the meaning of Memorial Day became clearer. I met a ton of military veterans who had lost friends in conflicts. I also met dozens of relatives of soldiers who had made the ultimate sacrifice in service to the country.
One of my proudest aspects while running for President was how many military veterans got behind the campaign. There were "Veterans for Yang" at many events with distinct paraphernalia. I received multiple challenge coins from soldiers from different services. Soldiers still approach me at airports and simply say, "Thank you." Which is ridiculous, because of course I should be thanking them. But it feels good that they sense a bond. I spoke to a group of West Point cadets recently and got a lot out of it; they were truly impressive young people.
A friend said to me a while ago, "If America is a religion, veterans are its clergy."
So, how is America?
Some very negative things are happening right now. The Trumpian Big Beautiful Bill that just passed the House takes an extreme winner-take-all economy and makes it worse by the numbers. Two embassy employees were killed in a hate crime driven by polarization and tribalism. Thousands of international students are being placed in limbo by a vindictive Federal government trying to enact discipline on a major university. Other countries are telling their citizens not to visit America out of safety concerns. AI is quickly transforming industries and the way our kids learn.
The American Dream has seen better days. It's certainly not clear that our children will be better off than the generation that came before them.
America remains a land of warm, caring, loyal souls who are willing to help their neighbors and lend a hand. I've seen this up close over the last number of years. I've met thousands of people of every walk of life in just about every environment you can think of, from the biker bar in Iowa to the church in South Carolina to the American Legion hall in New Hampshire to the tech HQ in California to the comedy club in Ohio. The vast majority of Americans - 98% - are good-natured and welcoming in person.
So why are we filled with such doubt about our shared future?
We are being let down by our systems. There are negative forces in our politics, our media, and definitely our social media such that instead of neighbors or friends, we see enemies and adversaries. This funhouse mirror is setting us up to fail.
This week I spoke to a military veteran who is considering running for Congress in a swing district. He is somewhat daunted by the tasks associated with running for office, particularly calling people to ask for money. He said, "It's funny, having served over a dozen years in the military I was certainly willing to undergo various risks for the country. There's less physical risk in running for office than what I was doing back then. But it still feels higher somehow."
I hope he runs. And if he does I'll try to help him.
To me, Memorial Day is not just about commemorating the sacrifices that servicemen and women have made on behalf of the country, but also a day to reflect on the best of America, the country we aspire to be. We still hold that promise and potential, to be the land that so many have died trying to protect. Getting there will take a lot of change from where we are now, but I know that it's possible. Funhouse mirrors aren't reality. I've met the best of us, and they are capable of great things.
To see what Forward is doing to cure our systems, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/3b22f4f3-4f3b-4156-8533-70643526944d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). clean_body: Hello, I hope that you're enjoying this weekend with friends and family. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | ---|---|--- * | | | May 26 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Hello, I hope that you're enjoying this weekend with friends and family. Memorial Day weekend is the unofficial beginning of Summer. Summer 2025 is here! How does it feel? I grew up in upstate New York and then a suburb of New York City in Westchester County. There were not many kids there from military families. A couple guys from my high school went to West Point or the Air Force Academy but they were somewhat unusual. Growing up, Memorial Day weekend simply meant barbecues and the beginning of summer movie season. This is not to say I wasn't patriotic - I've loved America my entire life. Perhaps that's the norm among children of immigrants. As a kid, I used to get into playful fights with my cousins because I would always say America was the best country on Earth. I founded an organization called Venture for America that I ran for six years. And of course, I ran for President because I thought I could help improve things for people. It was only after I ran for President that the meaning of Memorial Day became clearer. I met a ton of military veterans who had lost friends in conflicts. I also met dozens of relatives of soldiers who had made the ultimate sacrifice in service to the country. One of my proudest aspects while running for President was how many military veterans got behind the campaign. There were "Veterans for Yang" at many events with distinct paraphernalia. I received multiple challenge coins from soldiers from different services. Soldiers still approach me at airports and simply say, "Thank you." Which is ridiculous, because of course I should be thanking them. But it feels good that they sense a bond. I spoke to a group of West Point cadets recently and got a lot out of it; they were truly impressive young people. A friend said to me a while ago, "If America is a religion, veterans are its clergy." So, how is America? Some very negative things are happening right now. The Trumpian Big Beautiful Bill that just passed the House takes an extreme winner-take-all economy and makes it worse by the numbers. Two embassy employees were killed in a hate crime driven by polarization and tribalism. Thousands of international students are being placed in limbo by a vindictive Federal government trying to enact discipline on a major university. Other countries are telling their citizens not to visit America out of safety concerns. AI is quickly transforming industries and the way our kids learn. The American Dream has seen better days. It's certainly not clear that our children will be better off than the generation that came before them. America remains a land of warm, caring, loyal souls who are willing to help their neighbors and lend a hand. I've seen this up close over the last number of years. I've met thousands of people of every walk of life in just about every environment you can think of, from the biker bar in Iowa to the church in South Carolina to the American Legion hall in New Hampshire to the tech HQ in California to the comedy club in Ohio. The vast majority of Americans - 98% - are good-natured and welcoming in person. So why are we filled with such doubt about our shared future? We are being let down by our systems. There are negative forces in our politics, our media, and definitely our social media such that instead of neighbors or friends, we see enemies and adversaries. This funhouse mirror is setting us up to fail. This week I spoke to a military veteran who is considering running for Congress in a swing district. He is somewhat daunted by the tasks associated with running for office, particularly calling people to ask for money. He said, "It's funny, having served over a dozen years in the military I was certainly willing to undergo various risks for the country. There's less physical risk in running for office than what I was doing back then. But it still feels higher somehow." I hope he runs. And if he does I'll try to help him. To me, Memorial Day is not just about commemorating the sacrifices that servicemen and women have made on behalf of the country, but also a day to reflect on the best of America, the country we aspire to be. We still hold that promise and potential, to be the land that so many have died trying to protect. Getting there will take a lot of change from where we are now, but I know that it's possible. Funhouse mirrors aren't reality. I've met the best of us, and they are capable of great things. To see what Forward is doing to cure our systems, click . To hear my discussion of the 2028 Field with political writer Ross Barkan click . I hope the summer is off to a fine start for you and your family. | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Andrew Yang 111 Sutter Street, 7th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: From House Republicans
subject: Dear Alina Habba
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or
body:
Preview text here
Looking for 5 more MAGA Patriots...

[https://secure.winred.com/nrcc/alina-habba-thank-you/?amtposition=3&recurring=true&utm_campaign=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&ex_tid=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&utm_term=lrm-active]

THANK YOU ALINA HABBA!
[https://secure.winred.com/nrcc/alina-habba-thank-you/?amtposition=3&recurring=true&utm_campaign=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&ex_tid=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&utm_term=lrm-active]

Alina Habba is an amazing MAGA Champion! She has stolen our hearts
with her bravery, intelligence, and commitment to justice. As U.S.
Attorney, she's been a true champion of the law, standing up to the
radical Left and defending our nation's values.

And now, we want to show her just how much we appreciate her! WE'RE
ORGANIZING A MASSIVE GRASSROOTS EFFORT TO COLLECT 1 MILLION SIGNATURES
ON A THANK-YOU LETTER TO ALINA HABBA, AND WE WANT YOU TO BE A PART OF
IT!
[https://secure.winred.com/nrcc/alina-habba-thank-you/?amtposition=3&recurring=true&utm_campaign=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&ex_tid=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&utm_term=lrm-active]

READ & SIGN THE LETTER >>
[https://secure.winred.com/nrcc/alina-habba-thank-you/?amtposition=3&recurring=true&utm_campaign=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&ex_tid=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&utm_term=lrm-active]

By signing this letter, you'll be sending a huge thank you to Alina
Habba, telling her how much we admire and appreciate her dedication to
our country. You'll be joining a movement of patriots who are standing
up for what's right and fighting for the America we love.

So, let's show Alina Habba some love and add your name to our official
thank you letter. _WE NEED 7 MORE SIGNATURES FROM YOUR AREA BEFORE WE
CAN SEND IT OFF! _

THANK YOU ALINA HABBA!
[https://secure.winred.com/nrcc/alina-habba-thank-you/?amtposition=3&recurring=true&utm_campaign=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&ex_tid=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&utm_term=lrm-active]

#MAGA
[https://secure.winred.com/nrcc/alina-habba-thank-you/?amtposition=3&recurring=true&utm_campaign=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&ex_tid=20250522_WA90.116684_t1644560-573&utm_term=lrm-active]

Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or

320 First St SE
Washington, DC 20003

name: Chris Deluzio
subject: This Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
https://chrisforpa.com/ [https://chrisforpa.com/] Derek,

As a veteran, I’ve had the privilege of serving alongside some of the bravest men and women I’ve ever met who were willing to risk their all for the promise of America.

Today, on Memorial Day, we reflect on the Americans who gave this country the last full measure of devotion, who laid down their lives for the promise of America.

Here in Western Pennsylvania, I know many families have experienced the heartbreaking loss of a loved one in this way. So for those Gold Star families who carry the pain from someone in their lives who paid the ultimate price: I want you to know that we are immensely grateful for their service, and we will never forget their memory or their sacrifice.

This Memorial Day, I hope you’ll join me in pausing to remember the fallen heroes and honoring their bravery and unwavering devotion to our country.

In service,

Chris Deluzio
Make sure this email goes to your inbox. Add info@chrisforpa.com to your address book. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you no longer want to receive our messages, you can unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/z5DxLUzOH0Cbr-Ed1epOKA2?unsubscribedata=NOt0tvS%2F7b9vEnHpJyaqvunEZXvGCZ9I6cArOy0Qn2GNA58%2BC1XOUghuD5D7Dpx17RYvsRZBef66k48IGoduf3fldXuIl2cUKBw7UeW8D91FB6pB%2FkmLpatirxVPoa%2F5DYsjZxmFrl67cvrZoezb1%2B28IISCpz7FJgq6pyQeq0IR04j1auloseoeMMFMkTlXQ6qFeGzj4nN%2BFrrUxmu6g7fYO4PQ0En1GF2PX6fGAI8kYHOVtjHVWVh3X%2F2TLbH2fyvENB5xst1Tnub1PzRQ5uc1o9dk8gWwiAvzIT2N5jLwkYQrfEfLo22CqVn%2F1ST3SobGD9UI61QmLwDP8XGdp0bA7gsKmpiYguniKn6fQn8IkurfPj7rPnFnUtbSieEs9tV2NeqWf0jrOfDjFSLXZW7RfMzRoJOZT4ZmC64xGpenUSrWzcJDzaozcF3DXX2v .
Chris Deluzio is a United States veteran. Use of rank, title, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense, Department of the Navy or the Department of the Army.

name: The Republican Party in the Virgin Islands
subject: RPVI: Memorial Day Message
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican Party in the Virgin Islands
body:
We Honor the Fallen

| | | | | [|
---](https://republicanpartyinthevirginislands.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aec4429d4907d8f643606ded&id=59aaebf82b&e=4f81ceec6f)
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
---
| **Honoring the Fallen**
As the nation pauses to observe Memorial Day, the Republican Party in the Virgin Islands joins Americans across the globe in solemn remembrance of the brave men and women who gave their lives in defense of our freedom. This day is not simply a moment for reflection, it is a call to remember the cost of liberty and the valor of those who answered it with their lives.
From the hills of Korea to the jungles of Vietnam, from the sands of Iraq to the seas our Navy patrols, sons and daughters of the U.S Virgin Islands have stood shoulder to shoulder with their fellow Americans. And when the call came, they did not hesitate.
During the Korean War, 76 service members from the Virgin Islands made the ultimate sacrifice. In the Vietnam War, 15 Virgin Islanders lost their lives. In the years since, including during Operation Iraqi Freedom, more lives have been laid down in service to our flag and our nation.
Each number is a family forever changed. Each loss is a hero remembered. And each life is a reminder that our freedom is not free.
As we gather with loved ones or stand in quiet reverence this Memorial Day, let us not forget the legacy we inherit, one built on courage, sacrifice, and honor. We will never forget her fallen. Their memory lives on in our resolve to protect the liberty for which they died.
We encourage everyone to honor them by living lives worthy of their sacrifice.We honor them. Let us never forget.
---
|
---
|

**GOD BLESS AMERICA & THE U.S. VIRGIN ISLANDS!**
---

clean_body: We Honor the Fallen | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Honoring the Fallen As the nation pauses to observe Memorial Day, the Republican Party in the Virgin Islands joins Americans across the globe in solemn remembrance of the brave men and women who gave their lives in defense of our freedom. This day is not simply a moment for reflection, it is a call to remember the cost of liberty and the valor of those who answered it with their lives. From the hills of Korea to the jungles of Vietnam, from the sands of Iraq to the seas our Navy patrols, sons and daughters of the U.S Virgin Islands have stood shoulder to shoulder with their fellow Americans. And when the call came, they did not hesitate. During the Korean War, 76 service members from the Virgin Islands made the ultimate sacrifice. In the Vietnam War, 15 Virgin Islanders lost their lives. In the years since, including during Operation Iraqi Freedom, more lives have been laid down in service to our flag and our nation. Each number is a family forever changed. Each loss is a hero remembered. And each life is a reminder that our freedom is not free. As we gather with loved ones or stand in quiet reverence this Memorial Day, let us not forget the legacy we inherit, one built on courage, sacrifice, and honor. We will never forget her fallen. Their memory lives on in our resolve to protect the liberty for which they died. We encourage everyone to honor them by living lives worthy of their sacrifice.We honor them. Let us never forget. | | GOD BLESS AMERICA & THE U.S. VIRGIN ISLANDS! | | | | Paid for by the Republican Party in the Virgin Islands.Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.www.rpvi.org | | | | | | | | Republican Party in the Virgin Islands PO Box St Thomas, VI 00801 * USA or |

## Texans to Vote on Amendment Banning Noncitizen Voting
Texans will decide in November whether to add an amendment to their state constitution banning noncitizens from voting. If approved, the amendment would be added to the constitution’s language prohibiting voting by anyone under the age of 18, for anyone deemed mentally incompetent by a court, and convicted felons, according to Ballotpedia. In a bipartisan [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/jc6FrrVyp1ewPfiL2VB8sUC1gLRvEmEaVfCUs4hTRQU7-xP5NentPG4OL4c5SB4NifX7eK0Y4X49jwvlY_DH8VfB7UII7RIqi-hTMntgDbJqBgXLTUL_e8RmXcabNQ-ylE742R5MIVo6qkPhyiQkx5AEMe0uHEmz2lIgbYO_jzmsfFlBddWCUAnoxOuPSiXRR7ShoqbeUP8XWEplL8VrH7vlbN_rmTFBaDr6N96BGncmAzHLDF-u-qCEkD_kzusk1Hl_2OZ3UxOAECE2kveN1IIwC0pdcVLlYnazrCE_Hy0kdLMkXckh5lPKKsvw_Nxal2_wIT2YyoMQTdNKZtA/Dw8uPP61Mr1qI8DuB9sG12Z4vhKi4z4_/9)

This just in from the Scottish part of Alaska: The Highland Games will be returning to the Alaska State Fair. ## Massive Ticker-Tape Parade Coming to NYC to Celebrate Post-9/11 War Veterans – RedState
There’s an old saying: “Better late than never.” One could argue that this celebration is long overdue, but at least it’s coming now—Big Apple Mayor Eric Adams announced a fitting tribute to the warriors who have fought valiantly for our country in the post-9/11 years. ## Memorial Day: Medal of Honor recipients John Basilone and Hector Cafferata
I write military history posts every once in a while, to remind people to honor those who fought for our freedoms. Dear Peter, On this Memorial Day, we pause to honor those who gave their lives in service to this country. Their sacrifice was real, and the impact lives on in every family, every community, and every person who enjoys the freedoms they helped protect. Today is about remembering them and also choosing how we carry their legacy forward. That means protecting each other and working toward a country that reflects the values they believed in. Thank you for being part of that effort. Together, we are united. Unite the Country
| Unite the Country is working to flip the House blue in 2026 and deliver the GOP a humiliating defeat. We're building a grassroots movement strong enough to hold Donald Trump accountable, expose his broken promises, and protect the progress we’ve made over the past four years. name: Tim Kaine
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Today, I am thinking of the many brave service members who have sacrificed everything for our freedoms.
Their courage is remarkable, and the debt of gratitude we owe them immense. I’m so grateful for all those who have served our country.
I’m also thinking of our military families across America and the world. Their strength amid sacrifice and the support they offer our service members cannot be underestimated.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/4732335837597868785955356D7875466C5331792F51456C556754514764574B6F65634E35582B65547464734E524A714475554F425831745331526C686F612F/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-05-22%20at%206.23.10%E2%80%AFPM.png]
Photo: Arlington National Cemetery
This Memorial Day, I hope you also take a moment to reflect on the meaning of the day.
-Tim Kaine

Williamsburg, VA 23185

name: Bonnie Watson Coleman
subject: Happy Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bonnie Watson Coleman for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108384652/547758285/1328194270?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250526_ST_MemorialDay_6MO_TeamBWC_NA_NA_NA&refcode2=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTkyYjViMS0zODNhLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=efXU_vMIN6OvDK6_chbOyQzrDzxsFWlXMalDoo4Hh3g=&emci=b3ff2d22-2537-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=6a92b5b1-383a-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=30096098) | On this day, I hope you will reflect on the sacrifices made by our service members. Men and women who answered the call of duty and gave their lives to protect the liberties we enjoy today.

We can never forget the sons and daughters of New Jersey who put everything on the line to ensure we can speak freely, worship freely, vote without fear, love and marry freely, and provide our children and grandchildren with a better tomorrow.

Let’s honor the memories of those who lost their lives in the pursuit of those freedoms. Today, let’s remember that equality, democracy, and opportunity are worth fighting for.

Let’s stand together,

Bonnie
---
* * *

---
Bonnie Watson Coleman represents New Jersey's 12th district in Congress. She is a strong fighter for progressive values and has dedicated herself to the service of her community.

name: Lily Tang Williams for Congress
subject: Honor Our Fallen Heroes on Memorial Day
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lilytangwilliams/honor-our-fallen-heroes-on-memorial-day?e=c338950e03)
---
| |
---
| |
# Honor Our Fallen Heroes on Memorial Day
Our freedom is not free, so many had made ultimate sacrifice for it. Look at this heartbroken picture. We shall never forget our fallen heroes and their families.

We should not engage in endless foreign wars. War should be a last resort after exhausting all other options. Here is my foreign policy statement on my website: [Issues - Lily Tang Williams for Congress](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=fa559555b4&e=c338950e03)

John and I paid our respect to his ancestors buried in Charlestown NH.
His mothers’ side family came to US on the Mayflower. Later at the start of the French and Indian War, one of his relatives, Susannah Willard Johnston was sheltering at Fort #4 in Charlestown New Hampshire when kidnapped by an Abenaki war party allied with the French. On a forced march to Quebec to be sold to the French, she delivered a baby, which she named Elizabeth Captive Johnson. Years later she wrote of her experience and published a book about her experience, [which you learn about here](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=6f611a1536&e=c338950e03).

Susannah's husband James Johnston and family fought in the revolutionary war. Each year before Memorial Day, Charlestown puts a flag and medallion on each soldier’s grave who fought in the revolutionary war to honor our heroes.
My mother-in-law is a member of Daughters of Revolution (DAR). What a family I married into!

[Honor the Heroes of the American Revolution](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=690328c90a&e=c338950e03)

**Coming Up**

I will be traveling to FL from June 1 to 14, covering Jacksonville, Palm Beach, Ft. Lauderdale areas. Please reply to this email if you would like to meet up or have an event for me to attend.

[Please Donate Here](https://lilytangwilliams.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8317033781a524f5f640e7e3e&id=e3b78250cc&e=c338950e03)

Thanks!

Later at the start of the French and Indian War, one of his relatives, Susannah Willard Johnston was sheltering at Fort #4 in Charlestown New Hampshire when kidnapped by an Abenaki war party allied with the French. On a forced march to Quebec to be sold to the French, she delivered a baby, which she named Elizabeth Captive Johnson. Years later she wrote of her experience and published a book about her experience, . Susannah's husband James Johnston and family fought in the revolutionary war. Each year before Memorial Day, Charlestown puts a flag and medallion on each soldier’s grave who fought in the revolutionary war to honor our heroes. My mother-in-law is a member of Daughters of Revolution (DAR). What a family I married into! Coming Up I will be traveling to FL from June 1 to 14, covering Jacksonville, Palm Beach, Ft. Lauderdale areas. Please reply to this email if you would like to meet up or have an event for me to attend. Thanks! | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2025 LILY4CONGRESS COMMITTEE, All rights reserved.

name: Wes Moore
subject: Today, we honor the sacrifice of those who gave their lives to serve this country
body:
[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=84%2E1086043%2EN8v9mG ]Wes Moore for Governor[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1002&akid=84%2E1086043%2EN8v9mG ]Wes Moore for Governor

Peter, you may
know me as the current Governor of Maryland. But I didn’t come into this
role through politics. I’m an Army guy.

My military service taught me a lot. To put the safety of others over my
own, leave no one behind, and live “Mission First, People Always." Those
are the same values that drive my work as governor.

It’s why I do not take a single day for granted representing
Maryland.

When I was deployed to Afghanistan as a member of the 82nd Airborne
Division of the Army, I was honored to stand shoulder-to-shoulder with
some of the best soldiers in the world. It forged the strongest
friendships I have ever experienced.

Unfortunately, not all of my brothers and sisters made it home. There were
some who struggled with mental health and lost their lives to the
invisible wounds of war when they returned. And I will never forget their
sacrifice.

This Memorial Day, and every day, we must remember and honor the men and
women who gave their lives in service to this country. And we must honor
them with actions of our own.

Our fallen heroes lived lives of action. Their deeds speak louder than any
words we could share today.

To honor them, we need to live how they lived: fearlessly — courageously —
decisively.

Thank you and may God bless you,

Wes

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.wesmoore.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=84%2E1086043%2EN8v9mG
