name: Lateefah Simon
subject: Protecting our neighbors starts with knowledge
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lateefah for Congress
body:
https://www.lateefahsimon.com [https://www.lateefahsimon.com]
Friend,
As the Congresswoman for Oakland, Berkeley, Emeryville, Piedmont, Albany, Alameda, and San Leandro, I consider it both my privilege and duty to bring the voices and needs of working-class, diverse communities straight into the halls of power where people like us have historically been left behind.
I’ve been an organizer for over 30 years, so I know what it takes to bring communities together, hold the powerful accountable, and demand results.
That’s why I asked my team to create a quick form for you to fill out on what issues you’re most concerned about, how Congress can better serve you, and if you’ve been affected by the Trump administration. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/y_xac2qEAUuOlWCDgNCPdA2]
At every step — whether we’re fighting for affordable health care access, education programs, or accessible transit — I know that the people closest to the pain are also closest to the solutions, and we need to use every tool in the toolbox to create solutions. This survey will help us to hear directly from the folks who matter most: the organizers, caregivers, riders, and neighbors like you in our communities - so we can continue lifting up your voices in Congress.
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_las_email-25q2?refcode=x_250411_recurring_act&recurring=1]
I know you’ve got wisdom that can’t be found in any policy memo or campaign talking point. So please, Friend, take a minute or two to fill out this form. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/y_xac2qEAUuOlWCDgNCPdA2]
SHARE YOUR RESPONSE → [https://secure.ngpvan.com/y_xac2qEAUuOlWCDgNCPdA2]
Thank you for always showing up for me, for your community, and for real change.
— Lateefah Simon

name: PA Dems
subject: fighting for our freedoms
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party
body:
# [#]
Peter,

Today, we remember and honor the brave Americans who made the ultimate sacrifice for our Nation and our freedom.

As we celebrate today, let's remember the true meaning of sacrifice and the debt we owe to those who have served. Especially as our democratic freedoms are being threatened by those in power, we can’t take this for granted.

Every single day, we must commit to doing what we can to protect the freedoms they fought for and strive towards greater progress.

Wishing you all a safe, healthy, and happy Memorial Day,

PA Dems

name: USAF/ANG Veteran Troy Downing
subject: This Memorial Day, we remember why we serve
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Today, we pause as a Nation to honor the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice in defense of our freedoms.

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.) Peter,
On this solemn day, we pause as a Nation to honor the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend our freedoms. Memorial Day is not just a date on the calendar — **it’s a sacred reminder of the cost of liberty and the courage of those who gave everything for the country they loved.**
As a combat veteran, I’ve seen the bravery of those who wear the uniform firsthand. I’ve stood beside heroes who never made it home, and I carry their memories with me every day. We owe it to them, not just to remember, but to live lives worthy of their sacrifice.
Today, I’m not asking for your support or a donation. I simply want to thank you for standing with me, believing in this country, and helping preserve the values that so many died to protect.
Please take a moment to pray for the families of our fallen heroes and the future of this great Nation. With gratitude,
**Troy Downing** U.S. Congressman, Montana’s Second District Combat Veteran | America First Conservative | | |
---
| | Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
---

Troy Downing is a former member of the U.S. Air Force/Air National Guard. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Air Force or the Department of Defense.
---

Troy Downing For Congress
P.O. Box 20333
Billings, MT 59104

[Privacy Policy](https://trk.emails.troydowning._kVhTRDPEB3UAZm9JXcSnA)

To unsubscribe from Troy Downing for Congress, {{UnsubscribeLink}}

subject: Built for men who don't flinch...
body:
No flash. No fluff. Just a rugged tool...
| | | [](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21529/6374453/b30f6a9a3fd3c9a5c8afbcb81d37442845b403da/2b5c1827f536ca410664fed4565d70f9953250c1)

_May 26th, 2025_
---
| | |
---
| | Some guys talk tough. Others carry it.

The Steel River Executioner was built for one kind of man: **The kind who doesn’t back down.**
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21529/6374453/b30f6a9a3fd3c9a5c8afbcb81d37442845b403da/2b5c1827f536ca410664fed4565d70f9953250c1)
---
It’s a tactical folding knife with real presence — 9.125" of solid steel when opened, with a wicked recurved blade that doesn’t mess around. No flash. No fluff. Just a rugged tool that’s ready when you need it most. We’re giving away 200 of these in our first run to real Americans who actually carry — all we ask is that you cover a small shipping fee and tell us what you think. **This isn’t a collector’s piece. It’s a carry piece.**
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21529/6374453/b30f6a9a3fd3c9a5c8afbcb81d37442845b403da/2b5c1827f536ca410664fed4565d70f9953250c1)
---
We’ll ship it straight to your door. You test it. You carry it. You tell us how it holds up. First come, first served — and when they’re gone, that’s it. – Lou
Steel River Tactical P.S. We’re not making this offer public. You’re getting it because you’re the kind of guy we built this for.

name: Team Pramila
subject: Trump just renewed his push to end birthright citizenship
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
For more than 150 years, the 14th Amendment has guaranteed citizenship to anyone born in the United States.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250526-5085-footer&t=1&akid=5085%2E420207%2EkSbPNr)
Peter, Trump just renewed his push to end birthright citizenship.
**It’s a dangerous, authoritarian move that clearly violates the 14th Amendment — but they’re pressing on anyway:**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250526-1?refcode=em20250526-5085&amount=3&refcode2=5085_420207_kSbPNr&akid=5085%2E420207%2EkSbPNr)
For more than 150 years, the 14th Amendment has guaranteed citizenship to _anyone_ born in the United States. But Trump wants to overturn that Constitutional right and open the door to mass denaturalization and deportation.
**This is a full-on attack on our democracy, and we need to fight back NOW.[Will you rush a donation of $3 or more to support our progressive movement and help Pramila defend our rights?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250526-1?refcode=em20250526-5085&amount=3&refcode2=5085_420207_kSbPNr&akid=5085%2E420207%2EkSbPNr)**
**This is how authoritarianism takes hold:**
* Ignore the law and the facts
* Strip away fundamental rights from citizens and noncitizens alike
* Turn citizenship (or lack thereof) into a weapon to be used against political enemies

We can’t back down in the fight to preserve our democracy. That’s why Pramila’s working both inside _and_ outside of Congress to make sure our constitutional rights are upheld.
**But since Pramila doesn’t take a dime from corporate PACs, she’s counting on your grassroots support to fuel our movement for justice.[Will you chip in $3 or anything you can today to help Pramila keep up the fight?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250526-1?refcode=em20250526-5085&amount=3&refcode2=5085_420207_kSbPNr&akid=5085%2E420207%2EkSbPNr)**

In solidarity,
Team Pramila

name: AOC
subject: Hate is a trap that sinks us all.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
If you are unsure whether this is where you belong, I want to say that **you do**.
No matter if you know all the right words to say; no matter your race, religion, gender identity, or status; no matter if you disagree with me on some things.
If you are willing to fight for someone you don’t know — **you are welcome here**.
If you are willing to fight for working people regardless of who they are, how they identify, or where they come from — **you are welcome here**.
Here, everyone is valued. But we have to stand together.
We cannot be tempted to turn on our neighbors, or be fooled into thinking we are really that much different than a trans kid who wants to be accepted, or a dreamer striving for a better future.
**Hate is a trap that sinks us all**.
Our movement for real change for the working families of this country will have to push harder than ever these next four years.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250526.l_kS1W&refcode=em20250526-8940-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8940%2E2526796%2EiyoQII)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250526.l_kS1W&refcode=em20250526-8940-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=8940%2E2526796%2EiyoQII)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250526?refcode=em20250526-8940-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=8940_2526796_iyoQII&akid=8940%2E2526796%2EiyoQII)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250526-8940-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=8940%2E2526796%2EiyoQII)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=23&akid=8940%2E2526796%2EiyoQII) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=24&akid=8940%2E2526796%2EiyoQII) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=25&akid=8940%2E2526796%2EiyoQII)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)

To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
This Memorial Day, I’m remembering the brave service members who gave their lives in defense of our country — and holding close the families and loved ones they left behind.
In San Diego, we know firsthand the depth of sacrifice that military service can require. I hope you’ll join me in taking a moment today to honor those who died in service to our country.
We can never fully repay the debt we owe.
But we can honor our fallen heroes by supporting today’s military families and veterans — and by working to make sure every person who serves this country is treated with dignity, respect, and care.
Thanks for taking the time to reflect with me.
-Sara
Congresswoman Sara Jacobs
California’s 51st District
Sara Jacobs - Democrat, U.S. Congress [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89313/images/SDS/SJJ-emailheader1.png]
Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
Sara Jacobs for Congress
PO Box 120085
San Diego, CA 92112
United States

name: TN House Dems
subject: A Memorial Day Message from our Caucus
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tennessee House Democratic Caucus | Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer
body:
https://tnhousedems.com/ [https://tnhousedems.com/]
Peter,
On this Memorial Day, we honor the brave Tennesseans and Americans who gave their lives in service to our country. Their sacrifice is the reason we are able to enjoy the freedoms we hold dear.
Whether through quiet reflection or community remembrance, we carry with us the memory of those who never came home, and we stand with the families who carry that loss every day.
The best way we can honor their legacy is by building a Tennessee that reflects the values they fought to protect: justice, equality, and opportunity for all. That work continues – not just today, but every day.
Wishing you peace and reflection this Memorial Day.
With deep respect,
Tennessee House Democrats
Paid for by Tennessee House Democratic Caucus | Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer
Tennessee House Democratic Caucus
P. O. Box 198082
Nashville, TN 37219
United States

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Today, I’m thinking of every service member who never made it home, and of every family who loved them.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=2202%2E400544%2ExFq2H1)
Every Memorial Day, I think about my dad. He was a career Marine — thirty years in uniform — and one of the bravest people I’ve ever known. In Vietnam, he saved the lives of six fellow Marines, earning the Silver Star.
Although my family worried deeply about my dad while he was serving, in the end, we were lucky. My dad returned home from Vietnam, while so many others did not.
There are thousands of names etched into the Vietnam Veterans Memorial of service members who never returned. Each leaving a family who loved them, a community with an empty space. And there are even more whose stories live on only in the memories of those who loved them and those who fought beside them.
So today, I’m thinking of every service member who never made it home, and of every family who loved them.
However you choose to observe Memorial Day, I hope we all take a moment for reflection and show gratitude for those who sacrificed everything for our democracy and honor their legacy by committing to our democracy as well.
— Deb

name: Martini, H.
subject: Ensure what they fought to protect is not squandered
disclaimer_text: Paid for by No Dem Left Behind PAC Not Authorized by Any Federal Candidate or Candidate's Committee
body:
We have a window to ensure that the sacrifices of our veterans were not made in vain. | | [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvUWHo9tkdcudztgboR-91DqIjAlEtjxzxSzmkFF18zvXzT-w1zTtZlxgsQ822hyzEDTJ1UyZhVMTmnqe5Ol8s51PQBNL4LIUd0aQYxzFwcdxYYdnG6c6niovuERrKvpIERSNsHAZ-1vCJEsNO6LDYkjm9A_w2PnTJs7PNQBBFw8vLpdiq27bDjH2XbKeMd2nvDQVz743iAdPDcTF83f7V1o-KqAu3kfffVilBOXRmAW2xP8xKprLayGHFQajJ4qlIwE5WBX8ZWQUvwUG3NXutUO2KUu5I2FGqS5oidoTKKJlIJ5hF-TubAEU45AVzDL38cRCptS-LCwjLQZoGKbuIe/4gt/WrhkUTHvTQyona6qUoTZag/h0/h001.hf6pDxoUc6tlZU9y8s75b6K9Kxa1Aze_eTGexgP9iaI)
---
Friend, I hope this email finds you in the midst of a great Memorial Day. As we take time to relax and remember the sacrifices made by American soldiers I'd like to take a moment to reflect upon what these brave men and women laid it all on the line for. When our greatest generation stormed the shores of Western Europe, the Pacific, and North Africa it was because our Democratic system faced an existential and irreconcilable threat from a competing system of government: **_fascism_**. In fascism the peoples say is replaced with that of elites placed at the heights of finance, industry, military, and media/culture. Rights implicit to the American system such as free speech, voting, assembly, and to dignity are overruled by repression. **_In our current moment we face a fascist threat to our democracy and way of life unprecedented since the times of the 1930s and 40s._** We presently have a window to ensure that the sacrifices of our veterans were not made in vain. However, that window is rapidly closing... I'd like to leave with you a quote from President Kennedy, one I'm sure you may know, but one which we sadly here less and less in this day and age: "_Ask not what your country can do for you; ask what you can do for your country_ " So as you continue with your memorial day please not only reflect on the sacrifices made by our veterans but also on what you can do to ensure that what they fought to protect is not squandered. Hassan Martini Founder & Director No Dem Left Behind PAC
---

---
---

name: Governor Kelly Ayotte
subject: Honoring Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
body:
Join me in remembering those who gave their lives defending our freedoms.

| | | | | [|
---](https://kellyfornh.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b706db8cb07a975cbe2ecb8b7&id=25996ac9bc&e=38cb9f3df0)
| | | | | Friend,
Memorial Day is a time to pause and remember the brave men and women who gave their lives defending our freedom. We live in the greatest nation in the world thanks to their service and sacrifice.
This morning, I had the honor of attending the Manchester VA’s Memorial Day Ceremony with many of our veterans and their families. While we owe our fallen heroes a debt that can never be repaid, I’m proud of the way New Hampshire honors these men and women.
As you go about your day, I hope you’ll take a moment to remember those we’ve lost — and to think of the families who miss them every day. Freedom isn’t free, and today is about those who paid the ultimate price so the rest of us could live in peace.
Thank you for joining me today in remembering these brave service members.
All the best,
Kelly
---
| Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
---

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Happy Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support
body:
# [#]
Peter,

On this Memorial Day, let’s honor the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice for our country and ensure their legacy is never forgotten.

This Memorial Day, I’m recommitting to upholding the values and freedoms they fought and died for and ensuring that our men and women who are able to return home from the battlefield are taken care of by the country they served. That means fighting for their healthcare, adequate housing, and mental health services.

As a combat veteran myself, I’ll never stop working to serve our fallen heroes, veterans and their families. We owe them endless gratitude.

Please join me in honoring those who gave their lives for our country.

Thank you,

Mike Thompson
Authorized & Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support.mikethompsonforcongress.com/p/KK6Ox2NzGk6ccCSbCGuKYA2?unsubscribedata=ZEkBPpW1AfCovwS8xJi1vl6a7c8lROwy8bi2a93%2BdxVHm68Xw6mmSbWoB%2BqigfL%2FLkqYsOhlViIGNollOsvRT7KbXUR8IkK%2BBnT2s3sCR7voShowYCVo%2Bod5mIibV%2Bp81Z%2FZpNDh9QzwXwIoK9Uke%2BhAMa0E3Wlp49MJ9tXPq3sZPsy8tnSUMzvVqkD6tatJF%2FsLvtPEGNO%2BhrDBF64sxw1si%2BYFshrJyvHc%2FUjBOQtTBM0XFKb78aCogCSx%2FbOUogfB0c1U5kS71CxXlC%2BukVBPlM6HAzfi3r87E5nzhEK5L1bpPrYiyoPSthoXRFU%2FrkfPHYc8Dw3XgEj8k7nniwsfPgCQVCDqmQP8XMbs1CXP2WTC1ICAkbUSTrefzxClH3i%2Fs8S%2FPq4SyAMSF0KKUBU%2BoKMioUTEWi0763rs%2BEQ%3D Mike Thompson for Congress

body:
Response needed as soon as possible
[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=18897263c15f484ab41367a9ed07eeeb&_e=zKvg0nI5zGPyxo4Dwl_d4wsyCZg74bE-8xpLY-5WKzY_w7TRXwjJcz4pki2dGGKpFVRthqeuQgX_EHxrjcW97TOsqIgZP19BzkKwle1fVc0eQjeVLdJ-gWFEsWWqrlOcF652Ab6OR4GJ2rYCg1vfIa2D_aBRoqhhk9mKfXa6C2Myk1nr1XNr0fs0MVsyc7fodUd7MuS80mcgwoyPa846XXd2ieLIYtNpNXfmGo6BVuQfneN-8CEKExMwO-v3F8GOkpMsAo3w9j_WD18uY9LdXA8D4YBzkXhaQZYeyfLxqQqQdUPo0AV5-p91Ld3vecIVVYjujG2SDr5nHLJjfmRcpL7IMJwanKu6dsgR0TPWnWtK6CS2FaNuuixTI7I9g7K-HYU3N1vZjWrHZvKRAp5PmoeM-RCbOmtDhmrRxeJNN8Yy2d5kczDjz7w7bEPKJXpxMqip3fCSdTxI_FVGeo76Kk0nmou9YRM3tcsec3-26jf0Sl6Gid_s5ADQCS6BdGXI5Hv_oUIWLlzmRFBy0p40sA%3D%3D)

name: Dr. Kim Schrier
subject: What today means
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
Remembering our fallen heroes. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108402833/547805988/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYTA4NmNhZi01ODNhLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0il-bF4nWJXuIkkxtLC5RSgVaTFGGJDIJxOjLWfZVW4=&emci=d70e0f19-f737-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=aa086caf-583a-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter,

As we gather with family, friends, and neighbors this Memorial Day, I hope we can each take a moment to reflect on the meaning of today.

Memorial Day is more than the unofficial start of summer. It’s a time to honor the brave men and women who gave their lives in service to our country—**those who made the ultimate sacrifice so that we might live in freedom and peace.**

Across Washington’s 8th District and our nation, countless families carry the legacy of loved ones lost in uniform. Today, we hold them in our hearts and express our deepest gratitude. Whether you spend today visiting a cemetery, participating in a community memorial ceremony, or quietly remembering someone you’ve lost, I hope you’ll join me in pausing to reflect on the extraordinary courage and sacrifice of our fallen heroes and what they fought for.

Wishing you and your loved ones a safe and meaningful Memorial Day.

Warmly,

Kim

name: Dan Crenshaw
subject: The Story of a Gold Star Wife
body:
To miss someone profoundly is to wonder what life would be like if they were still there
| | | | ** ** **The Story of a Gold Star Wife**
---
|
---
| She wakes up from a pleasant dream, for once. Usually, they are nightmares. He was in it this time, as if nothing was wrong and life was just as it was. Boring. Perfectly boring. They were laughing about something, watching TV together just as they used to. For a split second she is still in the dream as she reaches her hand across the bed to touch him. But he isn’t there. She remembers now. And with that realization comes the gut-wrenching wave of grief that she is all too familiar with. Tears well up. She squeezes her eyes shut and clutches her fists tightly in front of her face. She wants to scream but she knows from experience it doesn’t really help, and it scares her son, who is still too young to understand what’s going on. His dad was killed in action a year ago, when he was just 2 years old. He misses him, she can tell, but it’s obvious he doesn’t know how to process death yet. By now, the calls and friendly check-ins have mostly stopped. It was amazing at first, when the community pulled together in the most incredible of ways. She was grateful. She truly didn’t have to worry about anything, whether it was groceries or work or even money. What the military didn’t provide was made up for by his fellow SEALs and their wives. But eventually people move on. They don’t forget, per se, but they go back to their routines. They come over and try to make conversation. She isn’t good at conversation anymore, so she tells them that she’s doing fine, even though she isn’t. They figure they should come by less and less. Only a couple still check in occasionally. She used to be close with his family, but grief does strange things to people, and they don’t talk much anymore except to arrange visits with her son. To miss someone profoundly is to wonder what life would be like if they were still there. To know that every moment would be better if they were back in your arms. Every morning would be normal, instead of a crushing blow to the soul. Every evening would be full of laughter, instead of just ... empty. Even some marital bickering would be welcome. What she would give, even for a fight over the obnoxious way he loaded the dishwasher. For those of us who have lost our brothers to war, we grieve. We miss them. We never forget. But we are not broken by it. We know this is the life we chose, to be on the front lines, to serve in the most dangerous of ways. Crazy, maybe, but true nonetheless. But for the Gold Star widow, she never pursued war. She was asked by her husband and her country to remain behind and remain strong, often not knowing whether or not her soulmate was even in danger. She learned to get through the nights while he was on deployment by imagining what he was doing, picturing him bored and playing Call of Duty instead of prepping for a mission. She had practice, given they had been through four deployments together. But now, his brothers in arms have moved on to the next deployment, and she is the only one that wakes up alone each morning. She gets no reprieve, no rest from the grief. She is the one who must endure. Another year goes by. She begins to function better and starts a foundation in her husband’s name. He was passionate about horses, growing up on a ranch, so she raises money to get disabled vets and special needs kids trained to ride. It helps a little, and it keeps her busy. It keeps him alive, in a small way. Being around the horses reminds her of him in a pleasant kind of way. She finds herself searching the horse’s gaze for some kind of sign from him, as if he might speak through them somehow. She still hasn’t cleaned out his closet. The smell of his clothes is all she has left. A couple more years go by, and she goes on a few dates. Nice guys, but no sparks fly. Not like the first time she met her husband. At first, she thinks she should date outside the military completely. Seems to make sense. But it’s impossible. These guys don’t get it, on any level. They are intimidated by her, never knowing what to say. Losing hope, she reconnects with an old teammate of her husband’s for drinks, and for the first time in a while she remembers what a connection feels like. It turns into more, and a year later they’re talking about marriage. She will always be her late husband’s wife, a reality that only her late husband’s teammate can understand. They marry, and she’s beginning to remember what joy feels like again. The pain is finally dulled, but it never really goes away. This is the real Memorial Day. It is the day that never goes away, with no real beginning and no end. It isn’t one day a year, but a constant ache deep down in the soul of those who understand what real sacrifice is. For everyone else it is only once a year, a time to recall that these sacrifices happened at all and that we should remember them. For too many, it’s just a day off work, a day to slash prices, a day to throw a party. For the Gold Star families, it’s a day they observe with a mix of gratitude and dread: grateful the country remembers, grateful for the opportunity to speak openly about their loss without getting an awkward look, but dreading the culmination of emotion the day brings with it. On this Memorial Day, may we hope that more Americans will remember, and understand what this day means for those who can’t ever forget. -Dan Crenshaw

clean_body: To miss someone profoundly is to wonder what life would be like if they were still there | | | | The Story of a Gold Star Wife | | She wakes up from a pleasant dream, for once. Usually, they are nightmares. He was in it this time, as if nothing was wrong and life was just as it was. Boring. Perfectly boring. They were laughing about something, watching TV together just as they used to. For a split second she is still in the dream as she reaches her hand across the bed to touch him. But he isn’t there. She remembers now. And with that realization comes the gut-wrenching wave of grief that she is all too familiar with. Tears well up. She squeezes her eyes shut and clutches her fists tightly in front of her face. She wants to scream but she knows from experience it doesn’t really help, and it scares her son, who is still too young to understand what’s going on. His dad was killed in action a year ago, when he was just 2 years old. He misses him, she can tell, but it’s obvious he doesn’t know how to process death yet. By now, the calls and friendly check-ins have mostly stopped. It was amazing at first, when the community pulled together in the most incredible of ways. She was grateful. She truly didn’t have to worry about anything, whether it was groceries or work or even money. What the military didn’t provide was made up for by his fellow SEALs and their wives. But eventually people move on. They don’t forget, per se, but they go back to their routines. They come over and try to make conversation. She isn’t good at conversation anymore, so she tells them that she’s doing fine, even though she isn’t. They figure they should come by less and less. Only a couple still check in occasionally. She used to be close with his family, but grief does strange things to people, and they don’t talk much anymore except to arrange visits with her son. To miss someone profoundly is to wonder what life would be like if they were still there. To know that every moment would be better if they were back in your arms. Every morning would be normal, instead of a crushing blow to the soul. Every evening would be full of laughter, instead of just ... empty. Even some marital bickering would be welcome. What she would give, even for a fight over the obnoxious way he loaded the dishwasher. For those of us who have lost our brothers to war, we grieve. We miss them. We never forget. But we are not broken by it. We know this is the life we chose, to be on the front lines, to serve in the most dangerous of ways. Crazy, maybe, but true nonetheless. But for the Gold Star widow, she never pursued war. She was asked by her husband and her country to remain behind and remain strong, often not knowing whether or not her soulmate was even in danger. She learned to get through the nights while he was on deployment by imagining what he was doing, picturing him bored and playing Call of Duty instead of prepping for a mission. She had practice, given they had been through four deployments together. But now, his brothers in arms have moved on to the next deployment, and she is the only one that wakes up alone each morning. She gets no reprieve, no rest from the grief. She is the one who must endure. Another year goes by. She begins to function better and starts a foundation in her husband’s name. He was passionate about horses, growing up on a ranch, so she raises money to get disabled vets and special needs kids trained to ride. It helps a little, and it keeps her busy. It keeps him alive, in a small way. Being around the horses reminds her of him in a pleasant kind of way. She finds herself searching the horse’s gaze for some kind of sign from him, as if he might speak through them somehow. She still hasn’t cleaned out his closet. The smell of his clothes is all she has left. A couple more years go by, and she goes on a few dates. Nice guys, but no sparks fly. Not like the first time she met her husband. At first, she thinks she should date outside the military completely. Seems to make sense. But it’s impossible. These guys don’t get it, on any level. They are intimidated by her, never knowing what to say. Losing hope, she reconnects with an old teammate of her husband’s for drinks, and for the first time in a while she remembers what a connection feels like. It turns into more, and a year later they’re talking about marriage. She will always be her late husband’s wife, a reality that only her late husband’s teammate can understand. They marry, and she’s beginning to remember what joy feels like again. The pain is finally dulled, but it never really goes away. This is the real Memorial Day. It is the day that never goes away, with no real beginning and no end. It isn’t one day a year, but a constant ache deep down in the soul of those who understand what real sacrifice is. For everyone else it is only once a year, a time to recall that these sacrifices happened at all and that we should remember them. For too many, it’s just a day off work, a day to slash prices, a day to throw a party. For the Gold Star families, it’s a day they observe with a mix of gratitude and dread: grateful the country remembers, grateful for the opportunity to speak openly about their loss without getting an awkward look, but dreading the culmination of emotion the day brings with it. On this Memorial Day, may we hope that more Americans will remember, and understand what this day means for those who can’t ever forget. -Dan Crenshaw | | | | | No longer want to receive these emails? . subject: This is about silencing grassroots power
body:
Trump and his cronies-turned-secretaries are weaponizing the Justice Department to target their political opponents. |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108403726/547809653/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE565_20250526_MayActBlue_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1056212&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjQzNmU1Ni01YjNhLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hpccQXUMrCkibZYZpgnhF4MX97UF_RYc6kf9L5Gv_oM=) Peter, two things are crystal clear:

First, ActBlue — the platform powering small-dollar donations for Democratic candidates and progressive causes (including EMILYs List!) — has been under threat ever since Donald Trump instructed his Justice Department to investigate it last month.

Second, Trump and his cronies-turned-department-secretaries are weaponizing the Justice Department to target their political opponents and dismantle the systems and organizations that oppose their dangerous agenda.

Trump, Pete Hegseth, Kash Patel, and others are threatening opponents, sowing chaos, and undermining the rule of law at every turn. This is not just about headlines. It's about weaponizing the power they hold and trying to demolish the infrastructure that powers the campaigns of Democratic pro-choice women across the country, up and down the ballot.

This is how an absolute ruler operates. They go after the money. And they try to break the tools we use to organize, communicate, and stand against them.

[**Peter, donate today to build grassroots power and help elect pro-choice women across the country.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108403727/547809656/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE565_20250526_MayActBlue_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1056212&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjQzNmU1Ni01YjNhLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hpccQXUMrCkibZYZpgnhF4MX97UF_RYc6kf9L5Gv_oM=) | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108403728/547809657/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE565_20250526_MayActBlue_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1056212&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjQzNmU1Ni01YjNhLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hpccQXUMrCkibZYZpgnhF4MX97UF_RYc6kf9L5Gv_oM=)

But your support helps us push back. It helps us stay ready, and sends a clear message:

**We're not afraid. And we're not going anywhere.**

They're afraid of us: The millions of women, voters, and activists standing up for choice, freedom, and democracy. That's why they're attacking our tools. That's why they're trying to shut us down.

clean_body: Trump and his cronies-turned-secretaries are weaponizing the Justice Department to target their political opponents. | = Peter, two things are crystal clear: First, ActBlue — the platform powering small-dollar donations for Democratic candidates and progressive causes (including EMILYs List!) — has been under threat ever since Donald Trump instructed his Justice Department to investigate it last month. Second, Trump and his cronies-turned-department-secretaries are weaponizing the Justice Department to target their political opponents and dismantle the systems and organizations that oppose their dangerous agenda. Trump, Pete Hegseth, Kash Patel, and others are threatening opponents, sowing chaos, and undermining the rule of law at every turn. This is not just about headlines. It's about weaponizing the power they hold and trying to demolish the infrastructure that powers the campaigns of Democratic pro-choice women across the country, up and down the ballot. This is how an absolute ruler operates. They go after the money. And they try to break the tools we use to organize, communicate, and stand against them. = | = Let's be clear: Trump and the MAGA right know that how they're governing is unpopular, so silencing our community is their strategy. But your support helps us push back. It helps us stay ready, and sends a clear message: We're not afraid. And we're not going anywhere. They're afraid of us: The millions of women, voters, and activists standing up for choice, freedom, and democracy. That's why they're attacking our tools. That's why they're trying to shut us down. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you for being with us in this fight, the EMILYs List Digital team = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. body:

Let’s not waste time: If we want to flip this Texas Senate seat, we need to start building our war chest right now .
👉 Chip in $10 or more before the end of month fundraising deadline to help us seize this once-in-a-generation opportunity » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052625_2]
Here’s what’s happening:
Ken Paxton is leading John Cornyn in the Republican primary. Yes — that Ken Paxton. The indicted, impeached, Trump-worshipping extremist whose scandals could fill a book.
And if (more like when ) he becomes the GOP nominee, Texas Democrats will have a gift we’ve never had before:
→ A deeply unpopular, scandal-ridden MAGA lunatic
→ Tied at the hip to Trump’s collapsing regime
→ Running on a record of economic disaster and authoritarianism
While Republicans tear each other to shreds, Democrats have a clear path — if we’re bold enough to take it.
We need to ensure our Democratic nominee enters this race with strength, momentum, and serious resources. That starts with grassroots donors like you stepping up NOW — before the big money floods in to rescue Paxton. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052625_2]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?express_lane=true&refcode=052625_2&amount=5]

Make no mistake: This isn’t just about Texas. This is about the future of the Senate. The future of our economy. The future of our democracy.
Trump and Paxton want to burn it all down. We can stop them — if we start fighting back today . [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=052625_2]
Let’s go.

## **On Episode 172 of The Qasim Rashid Show:**
* Color of Change Executive Director Portia Allen-Kyle and I discuss the state of racial justice in America, and how we can combat white supremacy.
* Kahlil Greene and I discuss the legacy of Malcolm X on his 100th Birthday.
* I ask you to join our movement as now more than 25 million people have read Let's Address This.

body:
Democracy is at risk of being replaced with what the greatest generation fought to destroy | | [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-6aIc5NVs3LvGaQ_g_h9uCKlhHLfQjivuVR0daIucZk4RYYahlNjEzQWmjFkPM1WTiDk-0cuL_5JjCi9M0svW7S3AhyvcutJR50XfmdDZFp0nQozqfflYe7UUBCjEnJCRezuifERq-2Jda8rJ-hm1QHJmjeJ-93xUPJ4tO2rvj9hpUaV9tgD0C4TIiHDxce0ihZA9W-0VAKaBmNfHf_b40zcfEM0eZm_IEoMvjB66UGkLnWCxaP8sl3JiwJDIhU6qhCiMpRqt9lNSDYR__2v63q4A42x8RmgLjmuUC084MvE8SwS893tyP3u1aHAGdBRZg/4gt/OcgFHZvFRkGL2LYFyfOcIw/h0/h001.jOWDq6UsXd0vtmhaNQnTIXmnaUn1o8jCmUrbtp4g4Ys)

Peter, When American soldiers died on the battlefields of Western Europe, North Africa, and the Pacific it was to protect our system of Democracy and the rights and freedoms ensured within it. Presently this system is under attack and at risk of being dismantled and replaced by the very system that the greatest generation fought to destroy: **Fascism**.
---
| [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-6aIc5NVs3LvGaQ_g_h9uCJWy9OC3C_OSWjg828P6b6Th6QHs9fzHaj6L8z7QKIFkRCFc2WW2r_XiP9-Svfw9g4sT6Ogg1ksTq40MdX2fu3_kd8XR0-ryZyGHXTPRlnSmMy3MPoz6Z-eofdOJsC52FL7ynaelvMhXSPEooyZUXapH9O6jdVTfxlFjO1fFvQqTBo9PBk4H0pu06Ijahmw0uHyM3qre9iACBl4kQq2NiciuB7waQEILUejB3sNciGG7Wy-yvUR2vPQzfQhmlfwmOcIB2uAL4SE3Q8KqXUte-yQYskLuEjsyrcLEHBpVGRsNQ/4gt/OcgFHZvFRkGL2LYFyfOcIw/h1/h001.PMjIbP_srsdbPJsTMdgJaBMV0OeGXmRsJ5h0e0CBGUk)
---
With a triumphant far-right controlling the House, Senate, and Presidency, the only thing standing in the way of the implementation of fascism in America are federal, state, and local courts who have taken issue with the constitutionality of the Project 2025 agenda. Until Democrats have their chance to reclaim the House in 2026, these courts are the only thing ensuring that our veterans' sacrifices were not made in vain and that the far-right cannot implement their fascist machinations. To clear the way of all resistance Republicans are working to eliminate hostile judges and are in the midst of trying to remove the Democratic majority on Pennsylvania's Supreme Court this 2025. **If they are successful this will ensure Pennsylvania never goes blue again, not in the 2026 midterms, and not when Trump seeks a third term in 2028. ** [We know you are in the midst of your Memorial Day but the GOP is hard at work and has already raised 1 billion dollars to put to work in the 2026 midterms and 2025 PA Supreme Court fight. Can we count on you to donate and help us protect the sacrifices our vets made to protect this country from fascism?](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-6aIc5NVs3LvGaQ_g_h9uCKnZLl0amtuAoPgVMb7PR90M0_ss9XCFILE9V5lLrYkDh22E7RKAv_bhtixVSiKjdfx7C5jqqWj2rjAdWxW0dnWHOpGp5JpJJIfT7keicFq-L4Iy3zClGiLt0HXycLagG7mkuCYATYtZJxJzKXWJKCSMXHijjeV63GGMR-y2G6Y8iF1Cezyfh6VZYJNgVW3s5jzqziOH79_d-penQlRpzM_PW-KNqO1Wd57umMM7UsqgepGGLTg-SJHqeMHuxbraHDqEV_DJMGyCQaODYc7kY363Ro2l5Uu9vvbTizIbO9nhg/4gt/OcgFHZvFRkGL2LYFyfOcIw/h2/h001.ehQWz-CeSaIgnK02P4bCwxaoKgfjDnue4DYk1pdFmDs)

clean_body: Democracy is at risk of being replaced with what the greatest generation fought to destroy | | Peter, When American soldiers died on the battlefields of Western Europe, North Africa, and the Pacific it was to protect our system of Democracy and the rights and freedoms ensured within it. Presently this system is under attack and at risk of being dismantled and replaced by the very system that the greatest generation fought to destroy: Fascism. | With a triumphant far-right controlling the House, Senate, and Presidency, the only thing standing in the way of the implementation of fascism in America are federal, state, and local courts who have taken issue with the constitutionality of the Project 2025 agenda. Until Democrats have their chance to reclaim the House in 2026, these courts are the only thing ensuring that our veterans' sacrifices were not made in vain and that the far-right cannot implement their fascist machinations. To clear the way of all resistance Republicans are working to eliminate hostile judges and are in the midst of trying to remove the Democratic majority on Pennsylvania's Supreme Court this 2025. If they are successful this will ensure Pennsylvania never goes blue again, not in the 2026 midterms, and not when Trump seeks a third term in 2028. | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Thank you and have a great rest of your Memorial Day. Team Defend our Courts | Mail checks to: Defend Our Courts P.O. Box 45569 Philadelphia, PA 19149 | | | Sent via . subject: Honoring our Fallen Heroes

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]

Never make a promise you don’t intend to keep.
These are words we all heard as kids – a basic principle of humanity and moral decency. President Trump and Congressional Republicans promised to lower costs, but continue to prove it was one they never intended to keep. Just this week:
* President Trump launched a global trade war. His on again, off again tariff taxes could cost the average family $3,800 more a year.

* House Republicans are advancing a bill to increase bank overdraft fees for consumers.

* Congress passed a budget resolution that slashes critical lifelines for working families in order to provide a massive tax cut for the ultra-wealthy.

Promises, without action, are just lies.
Johnny is working to expose the lies of extreme conservatives, who continue to put their billionaire friends and donors ahead of middle class Americans. Will you contribute today – anything you can spare – to help him? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]
He’s keeping his promise to always put his constituents first.

# ACTION ALERT: VOTE YES ON SB 125 AND SB 315
|
---
Our earlier Action Alert worked! SB 125 and SB 315 were voted favorably out of the Calendars Committee and are now scheduled for a **floor vote** on Tuesday, May 27!
|
---
# MAKE YOUR VOICE HEARD
|
---
### **EMAIL AND CALL YOUR STATE REPRESENTATIVE AND ASK THEM TO SUPPORT THESE PROTECTIVE BILLS!**
Use our public [House Directory](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/ty/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTU1NDg5MTY2MjY5ODc1MjQ1LFwiclwiOjE1NTQ4OTIwNzQ5Nzc4Njk0MH0iLCJzIjoiNGI1YjVhYjc4ZmEzOGZkYSJ9) to find contact info for **your** State Representative. **Not sure** who your State Representative is? Click [here](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/ty/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTU1NDg5MTY2Mjc2MTY2NzA1LFwiclwiOjE1NTQ4OTIwNzQ5Nzc4Njk0MH0iLCJzIjoiMDcwOTVhMWVjNjAzYWRkZiJ9), enter your address, and look for:
“Representative [Name]
Texas House District [Number]”
_Your State Rep is the second name on the list of results._
**Then use the linked directory to call and email.**
_[](mailto:kevin.sparks@senate.texas.gov)_
| |

|
---
### ****SAMPLE MESSAGE:
SUBJECT: Vote YES on SB 125 and SB 315
Dear Representative, I am writing to you today to ask you to please vote “yes” on SB 125 and SB 315 to protect Texans’ medical liberty! Thank you,
[YOUR NAME]
_[](mailto:kevin.sparks@senate.texas.gov)_
|

| |
---
|

# **[DOJ Officials Give Update On Current Investigation Into Biden Policy](http://trk.libertyledger.-YsLW1f-XJ3WaffsuM3Q)**

As questions mount over President Joe Biden’s use of an autopen for official signatures during periods of alleged mental unfitness, a new revelation has added gasoline to an already smoldering controversy. # **[WNBA Game Gets Intense](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
Tensions flared early and often Thursday night in what’s quickly becoming one of the WNBA’s most gripping new rivalries. Less than two days after their last nail-biting showdown, the Indiana Fever and Atlanta Dream brought that unfinished business right back to the hardwood. And this time, the sparks between Caitlin Clark and Rhyne Howard stole [...]

# **[Report Says Paramount To Moderate Show After Getting Streaming Rights](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9NzE3OTkifQ/FXwompmqtmuxsZZ-Lg2tww)**
---
In an age where streaming was once heralded as the pinnacle of modern convenience, collectors of physical media have been vindicated, and the South Park saga proves it. As South Park prepares to migrate from Max (formerly HBO Max) to Paramount+ on July 1, a troubling undercurrent has emerged: censorship. A number of episodes—around 15 [...]

# **[Trump Decision During Meeting Draw A Lot Of Reaction](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9NzE3OTkifQ/3lxyTUmln3fsFL1Qt9vt8A)**
---
In a moment that should have triggered national soul-searching, the conversation around South Africa’s white farmer crisis was instead drowned in predictable outrage—mostly directed at the man bold enough to bring it up: President Donald Trump. During a tense Oval Office meeting with South African President Cyril Ramaphosa, Trump didn’t mince words. He showed video [...]

name: Gina Ortiz Jones
subject: The Battle for Texas is on →
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio House Democratic Campaign Committee, 545 E Town St
body:
Huge update in San Antonio’s mayoral race:

|
---

body:
Remember. Honor. Never Forget.

| | | | [ ](https://oregon.gop/)
---
| | Friend, On this Memorial Day, we pause to remember the brave men and women who laid down their lives in defense of the United States of America. Freedom has never been free. It was purchased at the highest price by heroes who believed in something greater than themselves – the promise of liberty, justice, and a nation worth fighting for.
---
| | [ ](https://links.infooregongop.com/els/v2/_j6ZhkVAddJ~/NnMyZzg4QWU5aGhmeXpDYi9Wd0UrOGJmblhFbTdEK2FoVVdLRHh1OWQ5SGFZa0Y2Yk9EYm5qUFVHbTlNRWNRVVBjVTJnZVk4azA5aWxOcmlNMEhXRlRlQzlpclN4bFdFdkFSM3RyajllUkk9S0/)
---
| | As Oregonians, as Americans, we honor their memory today and every day. Let us never forget their sacrifice, and let us rededicate ourselves to preserving the ideals for which they gave their lives. Wishing you a meaningful and reflective Memorial Day. Thank you, Oregon Republican Party

## Illegal Alien Squatters Turned Her Home Purchase into a Nightmare
Cathy Lindberg Jarosz drove down the street of her newly purchased home but could not find her house. Lindberg, a lawyer from Maryland, had purchased the home in Destin, Florida, in April 2023 to be closer to her daughter who had just had a baby. ## Why Ohio has a say on California’s gasoline-car ban * WorldNetDaily * by Josh Williams, Real Clear Wire
Ford Mustang Mach-E In the coming days, the U.S. Senate will vote on whether to overturn a reckless decision by the EPA that grants California the power to ban the sale of new gas-powered cars and trucks. ## ‘Duck Dynasty’ Patriarch Phil Robertson Dead at 79 – RedState
Phil Robertson, the family patriarch of the popular A&E reality show "Duck Dynasty," passed away on Sunday at 79. Peter,
On Memorial Day, we come together to honor our fallen service members and pay homage to the spirit and bravery displayed by generations of Armed Forces members and their families.
The freedoms we enjoy today came at an extraordinary cost. When you see flags at half-staff, please take a moment to remember all of those who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our nation.
Let us honor these patriots by striving to uphold the values they fought to protect.
I'm grateful,
Tim Walz

name: Victory Mailings
subject: Free Course: The Fight to Defend the Free World
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj204ifivgwiqkvonhzjwc74uvd9c7o583ukr06r4zg85xn9hdy5li3h8ma9b0rnvt32tihokyj1xrvd4ov1b&__stmp=swvyab&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swvyab&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jdkf6wsxohs13ra5zxky4oicas1nqm6qyu5w9v2lc5ybds8ekr7dugkkpt1eaou0enkb0phdup8khw84lah7vikwj7oappfj988qixchmn5ltnf61qpocmjg56j46y7tnn3i0dkl2b67hjz8yxb0&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swvyab&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swvyab&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swvyab&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swvyab&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jdkf6wsxohs13ra5zxky4oick33dy249qrolkbsyiu8wxckcaygjbqrsw05u4jj2a9110jnyx13m7zjlefl36v8ny45zmo4j5l39d3dtyd7te9up3nslpmw3g471zrd1zwf8f8xkjchjpycvwm58&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swvyab&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swvyab&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

## Trophy Wives & Culture Wars: Trump's Graduation Speech from Hell
Over the weekend, Donald Trump gave a commencement address at West Point that sounded less like a salute to service and more like a Nuremberg Rally disguised in dress blues. Standing before the next generation of Army leaders, Trump didn't fake unity or humility--traits he possesses in much the same way as a colony of fire ants or a broken lawn chair.
No. Instead, Trump did what he does best: **He turned a solemn moment into** a screed of culture war bile, authoritarian chest-pounding and enough dick-measuring machismo to fill a fleet of tanks. He even managed to wedge in a demented warning to assembled cadets not to marry a "trophy wife," because...irony's been court-martialed for a code red? I'm sure Jake Tapper's working on the book right now.
Trump rambled through recycled lines about "dominating foes" and obliterating threats with the usual tone of a tool who spent more time watching _Rambo_ and than reading military strategy. Per Trump, the military's real enemy is...drag shows. Not even**the Bubonic-Plague-like suffering of Trump 's day: bone spurs**.
No, Trump wasn't there to inspire cadets. He was there to remind the base that the culture war's alive and well, and that he plans to use our military as sword and stage. Because...it's about me! Look at me! Pay attention to me! I have a gaping hole in the soul. Pleasssse like me!!
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/977d7422-c07e-4964-b5a3-c28df40f29cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Trump's idea of leadership isn't rooted in service or sacrifice. That's what makes him detest our military. Call them "suckers and losers," and ask his former Chief of Staff, Gen. John Kelly, "what was in it for them," while coldly staring at their graves. No, like tariffs, berating media and encouraging crowd violence, **it 's a domination play for a desperately weak man**. That and venality are all that animate this sad sack of discarded waste product with opposable thumbs (on very small hands).
Remember: **It. Is. Always. About. Him.** West Point was just his latest set and the cadets convenient props in his little authoritarian drama. So, while The Tapper Media will report this as just another Trump moment--because corporate media think their job's to pick up a shovel for the nearest stable, **those of us in indy media will tell the truth**. This was dangerous glorification of brute force wrapped in patriotism as farce.
Trump didn't give a speech at West Point--he issued a warning. That if he continues to hold onto untrammeled power, he won't just corrupt civilian institutions. _**He 'll attempt to turn the U.S. military into his own personal palace guard,** **a new MAGA iron fist for a new Iron Curtain.**_ And anyone still thinking this is just theater isn't paying attention. It's not just cosplay anymore. **It 's an attempted coup with choreography.**
* * *

### _**... Yo, Check This Out!**_
* **EU Tariff Delay** : President Trump announced a postponement of the proposed 50% tariffs on European Union goods, moving the implementation date from June 1 to July 9. I know, no way, right? I totally believed him this time!...He claimed this decision followed a constructive conversation with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, allowing additional time for trade negotiations. I'm gonna go with the more likely explanations: The crushed adderall pile high enough to focus an elephant ran out and he huddled in a corner like the coward he is, yet again, after manipulating the market and placing inside bets.
* **Criticism of Putin** : In response to Russia's intensified aerial assaults on Ukraine, Trump labeled President Putin as having "gone absolutely CRAZY." He condemned the attacks and warned Putin's ambitions in Ukraine could lead to Russia's downfall. Huh? I can't be sure, but **I think this is the first time he 's ever criticized Putin** so I gotta believe the call's coming in from HQ soon to knock Donny back in line and he'll be apologizing any moment now...
* **Harvard Grant Proposal** : Trump proposed reallocating $3 billion in federal grants from Harvard University, which he accused of anti-semitism, to support trade schools. I'm not sure if this was right before or right after he accepted a $400MM jet from Qatar, who happily housed the leaders of Hamas for a long time.

* * *
#### **🐱 **_**KITTY PIC!**_
OMG, It's Cherubic Steve! Seriously, tell me this doesn't look like something out of a Renaissance painting. I am beginning to think there is more to Steve than we know..
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/581bc189-8051-497e-86b5-70c210726097?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

#### 🎥 _**VIDEO ON DEMAND!**_
There's talk Noem's an "embarrassing liability." Not knowing habeas corpus, robotic answers in hearings ripped by Goldman, Swalwell, etc. Worst of all, pitching a reality show--immigrants competing to be citizens w/ Kristi as star!...There's only one star of this show! Other MAGA grifters sometimes forget this. And like the mob--which this is--you step outta line, well... IAC, we discussed what we know from an inside source!

name: Jackson Lahmeyer
subject: Saturday, I was in the ICU and on a Ventilator in Dallas but… God is GOOD and I am home in Tulsa today!
body:
Saturday, I was in the ICU and on a Ventilator in Dallas but... God is good and I am home in Tulsa today! I was traveling Saturday morning and collapse
| | [](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=21647632.11642.1.8acc1db29b60876a532d01ca5e7f1ccd) |
---
Saturday, I was in the **ICU and on a Ventilator in Dallas** but... **God is good and I am home in Tulsa today**! I was traveling Saturday morning and collapsed at the DFW airport (there is a miracle story here that I will share soon about me not making my other flight to NYC). I was taken to the hospital for an emergency surgery in Dallas which went smooth but there developed a complication during the surgery. I became unable to breathe properly on my own so I was placed on a ventilator and taken in the ICU where I was unresponsive for the evening. After several hours in the ICU, my friend, John Killian, drove from Tulsa to Dallas and showed up at the hospital and his voice woke me up which totally surprised the doctors and nurses because up to that point I was totally unresponsive and still not breathing properly. I think there might be a sermon somewhere in there about **the importance of a friend**. Long story short, I am well and recovering and no longer in a hospital in Dallas. So many have reached out and offered support and prayer.
Thank you all so much!
-Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer **UPCOMING EVENTS AT SHERDIAN.CHURCH** -Wednesday, June 4 @7pm: **FIRST WEDNESDAY** | Guest Speaker - Dr. Randy Caldwell -Thursday, June 5 @6pm: **Night Of Blessing w/Tim Tebow** | RSVP: [https://www.tickettailor.com/events/sheridanchurch/1665203](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=21647632.11643.1.516011f1771b5c08d519579e3ad75050) -Tuesday, June 24 @6:30pm: **Theology At The Pub** | Kilkenny's Irish Pub Please consider partnering w/the mission of [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=21647632.11644.1.46716d44a7d681d28c256bb8cfba1e3f) by visiting: [www.sheridan.church/give](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=21647632.11645.1.21d6392b18ed124244d512556c334b57)
---
| | [ Partner w/Sheridan.Church ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=21647632.11646.1.05d6349adaea3cc8979668911b3de516)
---
---
## The Reviews Are In!
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=21647632.11647.1.697186a8ce27ed6d81aaee3b5456b51b)

My **FIRST** book, ["_Chasing After The Wind_ "](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=21647632.11648.1.25fc47268bb289fd43d416c1c9141e61) is out! **I have been blown away by the response!** If you have not ordered your copy you can still get the **Limited Edition Signed Book** plus the **downloadable eBook** for tablets and phones as well as **theme song** as a bundled package here: _[*"Chasing After The Wind"*](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=21647632.11649.1.ca4e285f909490a5db1195f031bdd369)_ I really want to thank **MLB LEGEND Darryl Strawberry** for writing the Foreword and becoming vulnerable by sharing his story. clean_body: Saturday, I was in the ICU and on a Ventilator in Dallas but... God is good and I am home in Tulsa today! I was traveling Saturday morning and collapse | | | Saturday, I was in the ICU and on a Ventilator in Dallas but... God is good and I am home in Tulsa today! I was traveling Saturday morning and collapsed at the DFW airport (there is a miracle story here that I will share soon about me not making my other flight to NYC). I was taken to the hospital for an emergency surgery in Dallas which went smooth but there developed a complication during the surgery. I became unable to breathe properly on my own so I was placed on a ventilator and taken in the ICU where I was unresponsive for the evening. After several hours in the ICU, my friend, John Killian, drove from Tulsa to Dallas and showed up at the hospital and his voice woke me up which totally surprised the doctors and nurses because up to that point I was totally unresponsive and still not breathing properly. I think there might be a sermon somewhere in there about the importance of a friend. Long story short, I am well and recovering and no longer in a hospital in Dallas. So many have reached out and offered support and prayer. Thank you all so much! -Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer UPCOMING EVENTS AT SHERDIAN.CHURCH -Wednesday, June 4 @7pm: FIRST WEDNESDAY | Guest Speaker - Dr. Randy Caldwell -Thursday, June 5 @6pm: Night Of Blessing w/Tim Tebow | RSVP: -Tuesday, June 24 @6:30pm: Theology At The Pub | Kilkenny's Irish Pub Please consider partnering w/the mission of by visiting: | | The Reviews Are In! | My FIRST book, is out! But even after we earned a HISTORIC mandate to lead, Radical Left judges are stepping in and trying to stop us from deporting MS-13 gang members. That 's DESPICABLE. | [](https://substack.com/redirect/ba5bbbfe-8461-4fd4-8f1b-745aad4defb1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Many Democrats have not learned their lesson in the five years since protests and riots raged through American cities in anger over what happened with George Floyd.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..C4wlYCoSOVhEbpTV9Z7KnBBqZ87sHkuC21OzqTMo9Ak?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=164495897)
This Sunday saw many elected representatives and activists posting their respect for Floyd and said despite everything that was done to appease the violent movement, it has not been enough.
I go over how destructive the BLM riots were to Minneapolis, in order to make clear the following statements are asinine, in a video here.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/9297e831-19d7-4caa-8f29-4d1a5364e84a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Minnesota Governor Tim Walz **[fondly recalled](https://substack.com/redirect/ffc15d5d-6637-4f47-84c5-0b5a9ea8b702?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that people in his state "raised their voices, calling for justice and real, meaningful change to prevent a murder like this from ever happening again...Minnesota continues to mourn his loss and honor his legacy in the hopes that it might make our state better for generations to come."
Nothing in the statement mentions the week-long riots that ravaged the streets of his state. Granted, acknowledging such a disaster would only remind people how weak his leadership was at the time.
Rep. Ilhan Omar, who represents Minneapolis, **[said that in the five years since Floyd died](https://substack.com/redirect/8da9fb97-4288-4f01-82e3-013126709dd5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , "I am proud of the progress we've made, from organizing in our streets to the changes in local policy. I am grateful for the courage of our community in Minneapolis, and for the continued efforts of advocates fighting for justice every day. Because of these advocates, the world was forced to confront the violence embedded in our policing systems."
**A message from our sponsor (piece continues below)**
* * *
**[Together With AMAC](https://substack.com/redirect/1f4f0979-8eeb-45e1-9583-57f78c75d76c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **Tired of AARP? There 's a Better Option - And It's FREE**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1f4f0979-8eeb-45e1-9583-57f78c75d76c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
If you're 50 or older and looking for a conservative alternative to AARP, now's the perfect time to make the switch.
AMAC - the Association of Mature American Citizens - is America's fastest-growing senior organization, and right now, they're offering a **FREE membership** for a limited time. That's right - no credit card, no strings attached.
With AMAC, you'll be part of a community that shares your values: faith, family, and freedom. Members enjoy access to exclusive benefits, including:
* **Money-saving discounts** on travel, insurance, and everyday essentials
* The **AMAC Magazine** , packed with news and insights from a conservative perspective
* Updates on the issues that matter most to you - like Social Security, Medicare, Border Security, and more!
* A chance to get involved through **AMAC Action** , their grassroots advocacy network

Whether you're looking to save money, stay informed, or make a difference, AMAC is here for you - and now it won't cost you a dime to get started.
**[👉 Join the movement today and claim your free membership at](https://substack.com/redirect/1f4f0979-8eeb-45e1-9583-57f78c75d76c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Claim My Free Membership](https://substack.com/redirect/1f4f0979-8eeb-45e1-9583-57f78c75d76c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**(Continued)**
Nevermind that everyday crime in her city skyrocketed after the riots because the criminals knew the Minneapolis Police Department (MPD) were now handcuffed from doing its job, Omar said there is more to be done.
"Communities in Minneapolis and across this country continue to be over-policed and under-resourced. Too many families live with the fear that an encounter with law enforcement could turn deadly," she added.
Omar famously **[called to "completely dismantle" MPD](https://substack.com/redirect/1529bfef-b26d-46e2-a610-5ff802272564?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**, even though it was MPD, with the help from other agencies and the National Guard, who prevented the entire city from going up in flames. When presented with the option to do away with the police department, voters in the city understandably rejected the idea.
Rep. Monica McIver, the New Jersey politician who is facing charges for assaulting U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers, **[said"George Floyd was murdered](https://substack.com/redirect/8872a115-fe70-4877-a613-6599569b6dea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**--another Black life stolen by a broken system. That wasn't justice then, and it's not justice now.
PolitiBrawl is a reader-supported publication. To support our work, consider becoming a paid subscriber today.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..C4wlYCoSOVhEbpTV9Z7KnBBqZ87sHkuC21OzqTMo9Ak?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=164495897)
Not to be outdone, Alexander Soros, the son of George Soros and is in charge of his father's extensive left-wing networks, lamented how despite people calling for change "police killings have only gone up since, with 2024 being the deadliest year on record. Five years after, I hope this day can serve as a reminder that lasting change does not come quickly. I hope today can serve as a reminder to remember where you were and where you stood five years ago..."
It is crazy to think that the idea of defunding the police was a popular idea within the Democratic Party not so long ago. Even though it was a horrific position to be in favor of, there was a genuine force behind it that could have gone further if not for the massive riots that did not stop for the rest of the year.
It is because the monster the Left created caused problems no matter where it sprang up and was pushing ideas that were more insane than the last, people finally saw through the farce BLM was and said enough was enough.
It should not be surprising that Americans generally want police. Ask anyone who has been in less developed countries with police forces that are dubious at best. No organization is perfect, but the typical police department or sheriff's office in the U.S. is leagues better compared to the rest of the world.
While most of the Left want to continue looking back fondly on the "racial justice" movement in 2020, it is encouraging to see people point out the Left's version of that time period is nothing but revisionism.

Rep. Ilhan Omar said that in the five years since Floyd died, "I am proud of the progress we've made,... the world was forced to confront the violence embedded in our policing system..." | | | May 26| | | ∙| | Guest post ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Many Democrats have not learned their lesson in the five years since protests and riots raged through American cities in anger over what happened with George Floyd. This Sunday saw many elected representatives and activists posting their respect for Floyd and said despite everything that was done to appease the violent movement, it has not been enough. I go over how destructive the BLM riots were to Minneapolis, in order to make clear the following statements are asinine, in a video here. Minnesota Governor Tim Walz that people in his state "raised their voices, calling for justice and real, meaningful change to prevent a murder like this from ever happening again...Minnesota continues to mourn his loss and honor his legacy in the hopes that it might make our state better for generations to come." Nothing in the statement mentions the week-long riots that ravaged the streets of his state. Granted, acknowledging such a disaster would only remind people how weak his leadership was at the time. Rep. Ilhan Omar, who represents Minneapolis, , "I am proud of the progress we've made, from organizing in our streets to the changes in local policy. I am grateful for the courage of our community in Minneapolis, and for the continued efforts of advocates fighting for justice every day. Because of these advocates, the world was forced to confront the violence embedded in our policing systems." A message from our sponsor (piece continues below) Tired of AARP? There 's a Better Option - And It's FREE | | ---|---|--- If you're 50 or older and looking for a conservative alternative to AARP, now's the perfect time to make the switch. AMAC - the Association of Mature American Citizens - is America's fastest-growing senior organization, and right now, they're offering a FREE membership for a limited time. That's right - no credit card, no strings attached. With AMAC, you'll be part of a community that shares your values: faith, family, and freedom. Members enjoy access to exclusive benefits, including: Money-saving discounts on travel, insurance, and everyday essentials The AMAC Magazine , packed with news and insights from a conservative perspective Updates on the issues that matter most to you - like Social Security, Medicare, Border Security, and more! A chance to get involved through AMAC Action , their grassroots advocacy network Whether you're looking to save money, stay informed, or make a difference, AMAC is here for you - and now it won't cost you a dime to get started. (Continued) Nevermind that everyday crime in her city skyrocketed after the riots because the criminals knew the Minneapolis Police Department (MPD) were now handcuffed from doing its job, Omar said there is more to be done. "Communities in Minneapolis and across this country continue to be over-policed and under-resourced. Too many families live with the fear that an encounter with law enforcement could turn deadly," she added. Omar famously , even though it was MPD, with the help from other agencies and the National Guard, who prevented the entire city from going up in flames. When presented with the option to do away with the police department, voters in the city understandably rejected the idea. Rep. Monica McIver, the New Jersey politician who is facing charges for assaulting U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers, Black life stolen by a broken system. That wasn't justice then, and it's not justice now. PolitiBrawl is a reader-supported publication. To support our work, consider becoming a paid subscriber today. Not to be outdone, Alexander Soros, the son of George Soros and is in charge of his father's extensive left-wing networks, lamented how despite people calling for change "police killings have only gone up since, with 2024 being the deadliest year on record. Five years after, I hope this day can serve as a reminder that lasting change does not come quickly. I hope today can serve as a reminder to remember where you were and where you stood five years ago..." It is crazy to think that the idea of defunding the police was a popular idea within the Democratic Party not so long ago. Even though it was a horrific position to be in favor of, there was a genuine force behind it that could have gone further if not for the massive riots that did not stop for the rest of the year. It is because the monster the Left created caused problems no matter where it sprang up and was pushing ideas that were more insane than the last, people finally saw through the farce BLM was and said enough was enough. It should not be surprising that Americans generally want police. Ask anyone who has been in less developed countries with police forces that are dubious at best. No organization is perfect, but the typical police department or sheriff's office in the U.S. is leagues better compared to the rest of the world. While most of the Left want to continue looking back fondly on the "racial justice" movement in 2020, it is encouraging to see people point out the Left's version of that time period is nothing but revisionism. * PolitiBrawl is a reader-supported publication.
